batches.summary: 5-agent parallel audit of UNDER-COVERED multiplayer surfaces (Trivia Night/Duel/SameCards modes; Race + Map sync gameplay; Coop room flow shop/rest/mystery/rewards; Post-run persistence ELO/FSRS/Save/Analytics/SeasonLeaderboard; Workshop/LobbyBrowser/LAN/Identity/Profanity/Onboarding). Targeted to avoid overlap with prior MP-STEAM-AUDIT-2026-04-22 batches that focused on P2P transport, coop start handshake, lobby identity, and Tauri config.
topFindings: MP-AUDIT-2026-04-23-OPUS-A-TN-001 CRITICAL: destroyTriviaGame() never called — module-level state leaks across trivia-night sessions
MP-AUDIT-2026-04-23-OPUS-A-RC-001 CRITICAL: Race mode never registers mp:race:progress / mp:race:finish handlers
MP-AUDIT-2026-04-23-OPUS-A-CT-001 CRITICAL: Coop rest site actions (heal/study/meditate) are local-only — no barrier or partner notification
MP-AUDIT-2026-04-23-OPUS-A-CM-001 CRITICAL: Coop mystery events generated independently — players can see different events with incompatible effects
MP-AUDIT-2026-04-23-OPUS-A-EL-002 CRITICAL: Coop mode never applies ELO at run end
MP-AUDIT-2026-04-23-OPUS-A-SL-001 CRITICAL: Season leaderboard is read-only UI stub — no writer exists anywhere in codebase
batches.rootCauseNote: Two recurring root causes dominate: (1) seeded-RNG fork system is wired in name-only — many shop/reward/mystery code paths still call raw Math.random() so coop/same_cards diverge from turn 1; (2) several multiplayer modes (race, same_cards, trivia_night) skip initGameMessageHandlers() so their core message handlers are never registered. Together these explain a large fraction of 'lobbies form correctly but each player sees a different game' user reports.
batches.designClarification: User 2026-04-23: coop shops/card-rewards are ALLOWED to differ in current modes (no ranked/leaderboard runs exist yet). Future intent: coop = IDENTICAL, race/vs = VARIETY. Removed 6 findings (CS-001..CS-004, CW-001..CW-002). When ranked coop ships, re-open these against the seeded-fork wiring gap.

batches.summary: 5-agent ultrathink audit (Steam P2P transport, coop start flow, Tauri/build divergence, lobby identity, recent commits) triggered by user report 'coop on Steam: each player joins own game'

topFindings: BUG-1 CRITICAL: guest silently discards mode/deckId/houseRules from mp:lobby:start — smoking gun for 'each player joins their own coop game'
BUG-2 CRITICAL: initCoopSync gated on lobby.mode === 'coop'; guest with stale mode never subscribes to mp:coop:* messages
BUG-3 CRITICAL: host fires onGameStart after 3s regardless of ACK — commits host to a run the guest may never join
BUG-5 HIGH: SteamP2PTransport is single-peer; 3-4P coop/trivia_night cannot work on Steam
batches.rootCauseNote: Prime suspect for the reported 2P bug is the confluence of BUG-1+BUG-2+BUG-3. Fix BUG-1 alone (~10 line change at multiplayerLobbyService.ts:1562-1565) likely resolves the symptom.

batches.summary: Ultrathink audit of Steam/Tauri multiplayer after user report: coop lobbies form correctly but starting a coop run leaves each player in an isolated game. 4 parallel exploration agents (transport/networking, coop session sharing, Tauri/Steam divergence, leaderboard schema) surveyed the MP stack. 15 distinct issues catalogued: 1 primary critical (coop isolation on Steam P2P post-handshake), 1 secondary critical (subscription-after-send race on initial enemy snapshot), 5 high (reliability / protocol / test-coverage gaps), 6 medium (observability / architecture fragility), 2 low (config landmines).

issues.title: Duel/coop game message handlers never registered — `initGameMessageHandlers()` has zero production callers
issues.description: Ground-truth verified via grep: `initGameMessageHandlers` is exported from `multiplayerGameService.ts:926` but is only imported by `multiplayerGameService.test.ts:29`. It is never called from `CardApp.svelte`, `gameFlowController.ts`, `encounterBridge.ts`, or any runtime path. This function is what installs transport routers for `mp:duel:*` and the `mp:sync` fork-seed message. Without it, the `applyReceivedForkSeeds` handler at `multiplayerGameService.ts:949` is never wired, and duel/coop turn-resolution messages are silently dropped. This is the single biggest wiring gap in the coop start path. Race mode works because its messages go through a different listener set installed elsewhere.
issues.fixHypothesis: Call `initGameMessageHandlers(lobby.mode)` in `CardApp.svelte` onGameStart (~line 858) OR in `gameFlowController.startNewRun()` whenever `multiplayerMode` is coop/duel. Store the cleanup callback and invoke on run end.

issues.title: Duel state machine (`_duelState`) never initialised for coop — `initDuel`, `hostCreateSharedEnemy`, `hostStartNextTurn`, `submitDuelTurnAction` all orphan
issues.description: Grep confirms zero production callers for `initDuel` (line 658), `hostCreateSharedEnemy` (685), `submitDuelTurnAction` (711), `hostStartNextTurn` (1106) — all five functions are only referenced from `multiplayerGameService.test.ts`. Without `initDuel()`, `_duelState` stays null. `_broadcastEnemyState()` at line 1069 short-circuits on `if (!_duelState?.isHost || !_duelState.sharedEnemy) return;`. `hostResolveTurn()` at line 754 short-circuits on `if (!_duelState?.isHost) return null;`. Coop was designed to reuse the duel state machine per the comment at `multiplayerGameService.ts:956`, but the activation code was never written. Consequence: per-turn authoritative resolution never happens; each client runs its own local turn logic and only the partial `mp:coop:*` state replication (via `multiplayerCoopSync.ts`) patches HP/block back together — everything else diverges.
issues.fixHypothesis: In the coop/duel game-start path, after `initCoopSync(localPlayerId)` at `CardApp.svelte:858`, also call `initDuel(isHost, localPlayerId, opponentId)`. Host then calls `hostCreateSharedEnemy(templateId, floor, playerCount)` on each new encounter (replace encounterBridge.ts:805 createEnemy for coop path).

issues.title: `activeRunMode` always set to "multiplayer_race" for ANY multiplayer mode — coop/duel branches never taken
issues.description: In `gameFlowController.startNewRun()` at lines 434-438: `if (options?.multiplayerMode) { activeRunMode = "multiplayer_race"; ... }`. This overrides mode regardless of whether the lobby is race, coop, duel, or trivia. Downstream, only `if (activeRunMode === "multiplayer_race")` branches exist (line 1093), so race-progress broadcast (`startRaceProgressBroadcast`) is started even for coop sessions. This explains observed "each in their own game" feel: coop HUD shows race progress frames from the partner, not coop partner state, so the two clients visually appear to be running parallel solo races.
issues.fixHypothesis: Preserve the actual mode: `activeRunMode = options.multiplayerMode === "coop" ? "multiplayer_coop" : options.multiplayerMode === "duel" ? "multiplayer_duel" : "multiplayer_race"`. Add matching branches at line 1093+. Race-progress broadcast should only fire when mode is literal "race".

issues.title: Fork-seed synchronisation never broadcast for coop — only `same_cards` mode triggers `broadcastForkSeeds`
issues.description: In `multiplayerGameService.ts:615-617`, `collectForkSeeds()` + `broadcastForkSeeds(seeds)` is called exclusively inside the `same_cards` bootstrap function; there is no equivalent call in the coop game-start path. `applyReceivedForkSeeds` at line 949 is inside the handler registered by the (orphan) `initGameMessageHandlers`, so even if seeds were broadcast, the guest could not receive them. Consequence: each client independently forks its run RNG for labels like `relicRewards`, `enemyVariance`, `shopInventory`, `cardDraw`. Host and guest see different card draws mid-turn, different shop inventory, different reward RNG — classic "each in their own game" divergence. The shared run `seed` is broadcast (in `mp:lobby:start`) but not the per-fork caches.
issues.fixHypothesis: Either: (a) Broadcast fork seeds at coop encounter start — host calls `collectForkSeeds()` after `initRunRng(seed)`, sends via a new `mp:coop:fork_seeds` message, guest applies before any `getRunRng()` call; (b) Flip coop to full state-replication (host-authoritative, no guest-side RNG). (a) is faster + less bandwidth; (b) is more robust to future regressions. Requires `initGameMessageHandlers` to also be wired.

issues.title: Coop HP scaling (1.6× for 2P) not applied — `createEnemy` in coop path called without `playerCount` option
issues.description: `encounterBridge.ts:807` calls `createEnemy(ascensionTemplate, run.floor.currentFloor, { hpMultiplier: enemyHpMultiplier, difficultyVariance })` — no `playerCount` field. `enemyManager.ts:136` reads `playerCount` from options (defaults to 1) and computes `coopHpScale = getCoopHpMultiplier(playerCount)`. With playerCount defaulting to 1, the coop HP multiplier is 1.0×, not the designed 1.6×. The comment at `encounterBridge.ts:790` ("Canary HP multiplier is disabled in coop — shared enemy already uses getCoopHpMultiplier()") is technically correct that the solo canary multiplier is suppressed, but the coop multiplier is NEVER applied because playerCount is never passed. Net effect: coop enemies are 1.0× HP, same as solo — easy mode. Same issue for `getCoopBlockMultiplier` and `getCoopDamageCapMultiplier`. Verified by grep: `getCoopHpMultiplier` is referenced in `enemyManager.ts:85, 136` and documentation, but never invoked with a non-default `playerCount` from encounterBridge.
issues.fixHypothesis: Pass `playerCount: isCoopRun ? (run.multiplayerPlayerCount ?? 2) : 1` to `createEnemy`. Thread player count through from the lobby (number of players in `lobby.players`) into `run` state at run-start.

issues.title: coopEffects.ts entirely orphan — `initCoopEffects` / `processTurnActions` have zero runtime callers
issues.description: Grep verified: `initCoopEffects` and `processTurnActions` are defined at `coopEffects.ts:156` and `coopEffects.ts:191` respectively, referenced only in doc-strings and (in one test fixture). No `import { initCoopEffects }` anywhere in `src/`. The MP SKILL.md lists this file as "DONE + hardened" under the Co-op Effects row — false. Per comments at `coopEffects.ts:22-23`: "Call `initCoopEffects()` when a coop/duel encounter starts. Call `processTurnActions()` after both players submit their DuelTurnAction." Neither happens. Impact depends on what effects this service was meant to handle — likely coop-specific mechanics (shared block, redirect damage, etc.). Without audit of intent we cannot say what is missing gameplay-wise, only that the service is dead code.
issues.fixHypothesis: Decide intent: (a) if coopEffects.ts is aspirational, delete + remove from SKILL.md "DONE" table; (b) if needed, wire `initCoopEffects()` in CardApp onGameStart for coop mode and `processTurnActions()` at turn-resolve.

issues.title: Coop `awaitCoopEnemyReconcile` has no timeout — guest silently falls through on Steam P2P message loss
issues.description: `encounterBridge.ts:968-978`: guest calls `await awaitCoopEnemyReconcile()` inside a try/catch. If the host's `broadcastSharedEnemyState` at line 964 arrives before the guest's listener is registered, or is dropped by Steam P2P during early session handshake, the guest awaits forever; there is no race against a timeout. The try/catch only fires on explicit throw — the `Promise<SharedEnemySnapshot>` returned by `awaitCoopEnemyReconcile()` at `multiplayerCoopSync.ts:580` never rejects on its own. If the promise never resolves, the guest silently continues with its LOCAL enemy instance because the await is inside `startEncounterForRoom` which completes with the local enemy already created at line 807. Result: both clients enter the encounter with different enemy HP (guest=local-rolled variance, host=shared). Every subsequent turn diverges. This is the BUG-8 pattern ("server-relay-assumed") on Steam P2P: Fastify would re-relay on listener attach; Steam P2P has no such buffer.
issues.fixHypothesis: Add a 5-second timeout on `awaitCoopEnemyReconcile()`. On timeout, explicitly throw + log. Guest then sends `mp:coop:request_enemy_state` asking host to re-broadcast. Alternatively, have host re-broadcast snapshot every 500ms for first 3s of encounter; guest picks up whatever arrives last.

issues.title: Windows stdio redirect is a no-op in release builds — `let _ = file;` drops the handle, all Rust println/eprintln invisible
issues.description: `src-tauri/src/main.rs:60-67`: on Windows the log file is opened but immediately dropped via `let _ = file;`. Comment acknowledges "SetStdHandle via winapi crate would be cleaner". Combined with `#![cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), windows_subsystem = "windows")]` at line 2 which hides the console, this means every Steam callback diagnostic, every P2P send failure, every session-request log is lost to the void on Windows release. User cannot diagnose their coop bug from the Windows player's debug.log because it contains none of the Rust-side MP logs. macOS + Linux use `dup2` correctly.
issues.fixHypothesis: Add `windows-sys = { version = "0.52", features = ["Win32_System_Console"] }` and replace the no-op with `SetStdHandle(STD_OUTPUT_HANDLE, file_handle)` + `STD_ERROR_HANDLE`. Alternative: write a Rust-side logger that writes directly to the log file, bypassing stdio entirely (most robust; also fixes any Tauri-level stdio swallowing).

issues.title: Guest silently discards mode/deckId/houseRules from mp:lobby:start payload
issues.description: src/services/multiplayerLobbyService.ts:1196-1204 (host sends mode, deckId, houseRules, contentSelection, seed) vs :1562-1565 (guest types payload as { seed; contentSelection? } — mode/deckId/houseRules are never read) — Guest retains whatever lobby.mode was set by mp:lobby:settings. If settings was dropped (comment at :1201-1202 acknowledges this happens on Steam P2P), guest has stale mode. CardApp :857 then skips initCoopSync → guest never subscribes to mp:coop:* messages → host broadcasts land on zero listeners → each player plays an independent run.
issues.fixHypothesis: Widen the destructured payload type at :1562-1565 to include mode, deckId, houseRules. Apply each to _currentLobby before _fireSubscribers fires at :1589.

issues.title: initCoopSync gated on lobby.mode — guest never initialises co-op when mode is stale
issues.description: src/CardApp.svelte:857-859 — `if (lobby.mode === 'coop') initCoopSync(localPlayerId)` — When BUG-1 leaves mode stale, this guard evaluates false and the guest never subscribes to mp:coop:enemy_state, mp:coop:partner_state, mp:coop:turn_end_with_delta. Co-op is silently inert on the guest.
issues.fixHypothesis: After fixing BUG-1 this becomes correct by construction, but add a defensive assertion: if lobby.status === 'in_game' && lobby.mode === undefined, console.error and abort before starting the run.

issues.title: Host fires onGameStart after 3s timeout even when no guest ACKed
issues.description: src/services/multiplayerLobbyService.ts:1234-1242 — setTimeout(…, 3000) calls _fireSubscribers with pending ACKs still outstanding, only logging console.warn — On Steam P2P where guest ACK can be lost in transit, host proceeds into a live run while guest is still in lobby (or in a different run). Even when guest does eventually receive mp:lobby:start, both clients have committed to slightly different state and can't resync.
issues.fixHypothesis: Change the 3s fallback from 'fire anyway' to 'abort start, return host and remaining guests to lobby with a user-visible error'. Only fire onGameStart when every tracked ACK arrives (or all outstanding guests have observably left).

issues.title: Solo run save shares SAVE_KEY with MP run — solo navigation overwrites in-flight coop/duel snapshot
issues.description: runSaveService.ts:17 hardcodes `const SAVE_KEY = 'recall-rogue-active-run'` for ALL run modes. saveActiveRun()/loadActiveRun()/clearActiveRun() at lines 350/373/420 read & write that single key. If a player is in an active MP coop or duel and navigates back to the hub, then starts a solo run, saveActiveRun() (line 350) overwrites the persisted MP run state. On reconnect attempt, loadActiveRun() (line 373) deserializes the solo run as if it were the MP one, silently dropping coop fields (partner HP, shared enemy state, race progress). Coop partner who is mid-encounter sees their host vanish; rejoin loads stale solo data into MP context. Mode-namespacing the key would fix it.
issues.fixHypothesis: Namespace the storage key by run mode: `recall-rogue-active-run-${mode}` (solo vs multiplayer-race vs multiplayer-coop vs multiplayer-duel). loadActiveRun() chooses key based on the active session's known mode. Migration path: read old key once on first launch, route to per-mode key.

issues.title: Server `mp:lobby:start` accepts host start with NO server-side ready-state check — host can start while guests are not ready
issues.description: server/src/routes/mpLobbyWs.ts:250-268 case `mp:lobby:start` only verifies `currentLobby.hostId !== playerId` and updates status to in_game + broadcasts. There is no call to anything resembling `allPlayersReady(lobby)` or any per-player ready-bit check. A host (legitimate or malicious) can start the game unilaterally, dragging unready guests into the run. Guests have no server-enforced veto. Combined with the existing client-side ready UI, the server is the only anti-cheat surface left and it abdicates. (Note: pre-existing fix at commit 06e42b00a only addressed the start-guard for study-multi mode CLIENT-side, not server-side ready enforcement.)
issues.fixHypothesis: Add `if (!allPlayersReady(currentLobby)) { error 'NOT_ALL_READY'; break }` before updateLobbySettings. Implement allPlayersReady() in mpLobbyRegistry by walking lobby.connections values and checking a `ready: boolean` field (added to MpLobbyConnection). Add unit test for unready-start rejection.

issues.title: `{N}` template placeholder leak in quiz factAnswer and distractor pool (pop-culture numeric facts)
issues.description: Literal curly-brace strings like `"{125}"`, `"{1938}"`, `"{1.7}"`, `"{1984}"` ship as the canonical `factAnswer` AND 3 of 4 quiz choices for pop-culture numeric facts. Confirmed by FOUR independent testers in this batch: quiz-quality (C-001), fun-engagement (lowlight #2), study-temple (HIGH #1 — root cause: `StudyQuizOverlay.svelte` does not call `displayAnswer()` to strip bracket markers), full-run (C-001 confirmed 4th tester on `pc_3_instagram_facebook_acquisition`). Confirmed affected facts: `pc_0_mash_finale_viewers` (choices `["{193}", "125", "{1954}", "{59}"]`), `pc_2_superman_debut_year` (choices `["{2015}", "1938", "{3}", "{2008}"]`), `pc_2_spawn_sales_record`, `pc_3_instagram_facebook_acquisition`, Transformers 1984 study question. Hit rate across sampled facts ≈33%, suggesting the entire `pc_*` numeric-answer subcategory is broken. Bug lives in TWO layers: (1) upstream deck-build template-substitution pipeline leaves `{N}` in the source `factAnswer`, (2) `StudyQuizOverlay.svelte` does not wrap answer labels in `displayAnswer()` the way CardCombatOverlay/CardExpanded/ShopRoomOverlay/ChallengeQuizOverlay do. Steam-review fuel — a 30-second reviewer who screencaps `["{2015}", "1938", "{3}", "{2008}"]` wins the review thread.

bestEvidence.metric: 33% hit rate on sampled pc_* numeric-answer facts; 4 testers converged
bestEvidence.bugFile: src/ui/components/StudyQuizOverlay.svelte (missing displayAnswer), plus upstream deck-build pipeline

issues.title: mp:lobby:start handler does not apply payload.mode to _currentLobby — guest mode stays stale 'race' when coop was started
issues.description: In multiplayerLobbyService.ts, the mp:lobby:start sender includes mode at lines 1196-1204; the receiver at lines 1560-1590 only reads seed and contentSelection. The guest's _currentLobby.mode is initialized to placeholder 'race' at lines 602/712 on join and only upgraded when (a) Steam metadata read returned a real mode, or (b) mp:lobby:settings arrived. If both paths are late or lost, the guest's mode stays 'race' even when the host started coop. CardApp's onGameStart at src/CardApp.svelte:805-860 gates EVERY downstream behavior on lobby.mode: the check `if (lobby.mode === 'coop') initCoopSync(localPlayerId)` at line 857 never fires on the guest — no mp:coop:enemy_state listener is attached — host's broadcast lands in void — guest plays its own deterministic-seeded encounter. This is one of the highest-probability direct causes of the reported 'each in own game' symptom.
issues.suggestedFix: In the mp:lobby:start handler (receiver at ~line 1560), apply payload.mode, payload.houseRules, and payload.deckId to _currentLobby BEFORE firing subscribers. Add a console.warn if mode mismatch is detected between payload and current lobby state.

issues.title: Duel and Trivia Night start paths are never wired in CardApp.svelte onGameStart — both modes silently run as parallel solo games
issues.description: In src/CardApp.svelte:805-860, onGameStart only calls initMapNodeSync (always) and initCoopSync when mode === 'coop'. There is no `if (lobby.mode === 'duel') initDuel(isHost(), localPlayerId, opponentId)` and no `if (lobby.mode === 'trivia_night') initTrivia(...)`. multiplayerGameService.initDuel and triviaNightService.start* exist but no onGameStart consumer calls them. Starting a duel or trivia game on Steam puts both players into solo runs that share only the seed. User confirmed coop is broken; duel and trivia are silently broken by the same omission.
issues.suggestedFix: Extend the onGameStart subscriber switch to cover all 5 modes. Add a regression test that asserts each mode dispatches its specific init function when onGameStart fires.

issues.title: hasSteam is a module-load-time snapshot in platformService.ts — nine lobby service guards freeze false on late Tauri injection, routing Steam build through non-Steam transport
issues.description: platformService.ts:30-54 computes hasSteam by checking __TAURI_INTERNALS__ at module-evaluation time. In packaged Steam builds where <script type='module'> evaluates before Tauri's bridge injection, hasSteam = false is frozen. multiplayerLobbyService.ts consumes hasSteam at nine points (lines 481, 507, 519, 578, 621, 690, 734, 822, 832, 1657-1664). When false, lines 633-635/740-742 call transport.connect() with a numeric lobbyId string — Steam will attempt P2P to a non-peer — every sendP2PMessage returns false, retries silently exhaust. Host never sees guest; guest never sees mp:lobby:start. Each player ends up alone. This is one of the highest-probability root causes for the user-reported symptom on Steam builds.

bestEvidence.failMode: hasSteam frozen false → non-Steam transport selected → P2P sends to numeric lobbyId → silent exhaustion

issues.suggestedFix: Replace every hasSteam reference in multiplayerLobbyService.ts with a live isTauriRuntime() check (already inlined in pickBackend() at line 1850). Export a single live helper from platformService.ts instead of a snapshot constant.

issues.title: Stale rr-lan-server localStorage in Tauri WebView survives across sessions — pickBackend() returns webBackend, bypassing Steam P2P entirely
issues.description: Tauri WebView2/WKWebView shares localStorage between dev and packaged builds (same origin). Two Steam clients on the same machine share the WebView2 user-data-dir. If a developer ever called setLanServerUrl() in dev, or one Steam client toggled LAN mode then exited, isLanMode() returns true forever. pickBackend() at multiplayerLobbyService.ts:1846 returns webBackend; createTransport() returns WebSocketTransport. Both players hit the dev's LAN URL (which doesn't exist on the player's machine) → WS reconnect storm → no messages delivered → each player ends up solo. No UI indicator shows LAN mode active. clearLanModeOnHubEntry only fires on Hub entry, which a player going straight to MP never triggers.

bestEvidence.clearSite: clearLanModeOnHubEntry — fires only on Hub entry

issues.suggestedFix: In pickBackend, ignore LAN mode unless an explicit ?lan=1 URL flag is also present, OR clear LAN-mode localStorage on every Steam-build cold start in main.ts before lobby service initializes.

issues.title: Fastify webBackend mp:lobby:start broadcast strips mode, contentSelection, houseRules, and players from payload — guest CardApp aborts with 'received without contentSelection'
issues.description: server/src/routes/mpLobbyWs.ts:240-244 hardcodes payload: { lobbyId, seed: msg.payload?.['seed'] ?? null } — discarding the host's full start payload. The guest's mp:lobby:start handler at multiplayerLobbyService.ts:1560 sees payload.contentSelection === undefined. CardApp's onGameStart subscriber at src/CardApp.svelte:812-821 explicitly aborts with 'received without contentSelection' and returns to lobby screen. The host's seed-ACK retry loop hangs forever waiting for a dead guest. Steam P2P preserves the full payload because it bypasses the server. This means web/LAN multiplayer game-start has been silently broken since contentSelection was added to the payload.

bestEvidence.consequence: src/CardApp.svelte:812-821 aborts onGameStart when contentSelection missing

issues.suggestedFix: Have mpLobbyWs.ts:240 forward msg.payload verbatim (it is already gated as trusted-host). The server should relay rather than reconstruct the start payload.

issues.title: Two Steam clients on the same machine sharing one Steam account — host never receives second peer's LobbyChatUpdate.Entered event, lobby appears populated but no P2P session opens
issues.description: When testing with two Steam instances on one machine using the same Steam account, Steamworks' steam_get_lobby_members sees the host as the only member since both clients report the same SteamUser::steam_id(). The guest's 'join' is a no-op in Steamworks (already in lobby). LobbyChatUpdate.Entered for a second peer never fires; session_request_callback never fires. Both clients sit in lobbies that look populated (showing self twice) but never exchange mp:coop:* messages. This alone could fully explain the reported symptom when developers test with a shared Steam account on one machine, or when two instances of the Steam client run on the same dev machine.

bestEvidence.steamCallback: src-tauri/src/steam.rs session_request_callback — never fires when both clients share same steam_id
bestEvidence.lobbyMembers: steam_get_lobby_members returns only one unique member when steam_ids match
bestEvidence.testingReality: Single-machine two-client testing requires two distinct Steam accounts on separate machines to be valid

issues.suggestedFix: Assert at create/join time that localSteamId does not match any existing member SteamId. Show a player-facing error if a second client launches with the same Steam account. Document in SKILL.md that Steam P2P testing requires two distinct Steam accounts.
issues.note: [2026-04-23] assertLocalSteamIdUniqueInLobby() surfaces dup SteamID error

issues.title: mp:lobby:start_ack is not in MultiplayerMessageType union — sent via `as` cast
issues.description: src/services/multiplayerLobbyService.ts:1578-1586 — the TODO at :1581 admits the cast is a workaround; transport routing of unknown types is untested on Steam — If the transport's type-narrowed routing treats unknown types differently across backends, the ACK can be silently dropped on Steam while working on BroadcastChannel — explaining why dev mode works and Steam doesn't.
issues.fixHypothesis: Add 'mp:lobby:start_ack' to MultiplayerMessageType and remove the cast. Regenerate any type guards.

issues.title: SteamP2PTransport is single-peer by design
issues.description: src/services/multiplayerTransport.ts:502 (`private peerId = ''`), :550 (`this.peerId = peerId`), :724 (`sendP2PMessage(this.peerId, …)`) — 3-4 player co-op and trivia_night modes cannot work on Steam. Connecting to a second guest overwrites the first. Not the cause of the reported 2P bug but guaranteed to break any multi-peer mode.
issues.fixHypothesis: Refactor to `private peers = new Map<peerId, SessionState>()`. On send, broadcast to all peers in the map. On connect, add the peer rather than replace.
issues.note: Single-peer guard added: connect() to a second peer while already bound now warns and returns early (prevents silent session overwrite). Architectural multi-peer refactor remains out of scope. | [2026-04-23] Observability added; single-peer architecture unchanged; Map<peerId,SessionState> deferred

issues.title: initCoopSync called AFTER startNewRun synchronously transitions screen
issues.description: src/CardApp.svelte:849 (startNewRun) → synchronous currentScreen.set in gameFlowController :1135/:1156 → :858 (initCoopSync) — Combat scene can mount and the host can broadcast mp:coop:enemy_state before the guest's listener is registered. First snapshot lost → guest enters combat with no shared enemy reference.
issues.fixHypothesis: Move initCoopSync, initMapNodeSync, onPartnerStateUpdate registrations BEFORE startNewRun in the game-start callback.

issues.title: onPartnerStateUpdate subscription registered AFTER startNewRun
issues.description: src/CardApp.svelte:873 — registered at the bottom of the $effect, after startNewRun and initCoopSync — Same timing window as BUG-6 for partner HP state. First mp:coop:partner_state from host can land before guest subscribes.
issues.fixHypothesis: Hoist onPartnerStateUpdate registration above startNewRun inside the same $effect.

issues.title: Host never self-loopbacks its own mp:lobby:start on Steam P2P
issues.description: src/services/multiplayerTransport.ts — no self-echo in SteamP2PTransport. Contrast with :983-995 BroadcastChannelTransport which does receive its own broadcast (then filters senderId self). — On broadcast mode, the host's mp:lobby:start handler fires from its own echo, going through the unified handler path. On Steam, the host's _fireSubscribers only runs via the 3s timeout fallback (BUG-3) or on ACK. Host and guest take different code paths into the run — another source of divergence.
issues.fixHypothesis: Host-side: fire _fireSubscribers(_gameStartSubscribers, seed, _currentLobby) immediately after transport.send('mp:lobby:start', …) rather than relying on echo or timeout.

issues.title: acceptP2PSession returning false logs but state still transitions to connected
issues.description: src/services/multiplayerTransport.ts:563-575 — State machine lies: transport reports 'connected' while the P2P session is half-open. Subsequent sends execute the happy path and are dropped at the Rust layer; TS side sees no error.
issues.fixHypothesis: On acceptP2PSession false, enter an 'error' or 'handshake_failed' state. Retry the accept with exponential backoff (2-3 attempts) before giving up. Do not transition to 'connected' until a session is actually open.
issues.note: acceptP2PSession false now stays in error state, drops preSendBuffer. PASS1-BUG-9 fix in multiplayerTransport.ts.

issues.title: No initial broadcastPartnerState at encounter start
issues.description: src/services/encounterBridge.ts — broadcastPartnerState is only called at end-of-turn (~:1640-1654 per sub-agent-5; verify before fix) — Guest's initial HP/block never reaches host until the guest completes turn 1. MultiplayerHUD renders empty/stale partner state through the first turn.
issues.fixHypothesis: Call broadcastPartnerState once at encounter-start from encounterBridge init, with the initial player snapshot.

issues.title: $effect cleanup tears down all coop subscriptions unconditionally
issues.description: src/CardApp.svelte:896-906 — destroyCoopSync(), unsubPartner(), destroyMapNodeSync() all fire on any $effect re-run — If anything the $effect tracks reactively changes mid-run (localPlayerId refresh, lobby reference swap), the cleanup fires and kills every transport listener. Re-registration happens in the next $effect tick but any in-flight messages are lost.
issues.fixHypothesis: Either split the effect into one-shot 'mount' registrations (plain onMount) and reactive updates, or narrow the $effect's tracked deps so it doesn't re-fire mid-run.
issues.qaNote: Code works today (no reactive reads in $effect) but fix hypothesis (split into onMount) not implemented. Latent fragility — adding any reactive read to $effect at CardApp.svelte:818 would cause mid-run teardown.

issues.title: ACK retry loop does not await send-delivery confirmation
issues.description: src/services/multiplayerLobbyService.ts:1224-1232 — setInterval calls transport.send every 750ms. transport.send in SteamP2PTransport returns before _sendWithRetry completes (:673-707). — The 750ms retry can't tell whether the prior send landed. Three retries can fire concurrently while the first is still internally retrying, amplifying Steam network load.
issues.fixHypothesis: Promisify _sendWithRetry so send() returns a Promise<boolean>. The retry loop awaits the prior send before issuing the next.
issues.note: [2026-04-23] [2026-04-23 verify] NOT FIXED by 8f5d7a74b — explicitly deferred; requires transport.send() Promise<boolean> refactor

issues.title: Only contentSelection has a presence guard — other start-payload fields pass silently
issues.description: src/CardApp.svelte:812-821 — guards only against `!lobby.contentSelection` — A run starts with stale or undefined mode/deckId/houseRules and nothing warns. Paired with BUG-1, this is why the coop divergence is silent.
issues.fixHypothesis: Extend the guard to assert lobby.mode, lobby.selectedDeckId, lobby.houseRules are present (appropriate to mode). Log console.error and return to lobby on mismatch.

issues.title: Tag magnetism draw bias uses unseeded `Math.random()` — diverges hands mid-turn
issues.description: `deckManager.ts:198` uses raw `Math.random() < chance` to decide whether to swap a drawn card to a chain-matching color. In coop, both clients run this independently on their own draws (or on the same draws if the card pool is synced), producing different "magnetised" cards. Result: host and guest have different hands. Verified by grep of `Math.random` in the services directory.
issues.fixHypothesis: Replace with `getRunRng("cardDraw").next() < chance` (or dedicated fork label `tagMagnetism`). Requires fork-seed sync bug to also be fixed for effect.

issues.title: Transmute mechanic selection uses unseeded `Math.random()` — different replacement mechanics on host vs guest
issues.description: `turnManager.ts:608` uses `Math.floor(Math.random() * pool.length)` to pick a new mechanic when Transmute resolves. In coop both clients execute the resolver independently (because per-turn authoritative resolution is broken — see duel-state-not-initialised issue), producing different mechanic types. Hand visual state diverges. Player A sees they got "Strike" from transmute, Player B sees Player A got "Block".
issues.fixHypothesis: Use `getRunRng("cardEffects").next()` and seeded `Math.floor(rng.next() * pool.length)`. Requires fork-seed sync fix to be effective in coop.

issues.title: Naive `.sort(() => Math.random() - 0.5)` shuffle in debuff target selection — different targets on each client
issues.description: `turnManager.ts:1665, 1670, 1682, 2991` all use the anti-pattern `.sort(() => Math.random() - 0.5)` for shuffles. This is both biased (not a uniform permutation) and unseeded. Used in enemy debuff target selection and hand shuffles. In coop, different target choices on each client → enemy applies debuffs to different cards in each client's view → state replication via `mp:coop:enemy_state` has to paper over the divergence each turn, producing snap/flash glitches and dropped debuffs.
issues.fixHypothesis: Replace all four sites with `seededShuffled(array, getRunRng("debuffTarget"))` using a Fisher-Yates shuffle from `seededRng.ts`.

issues.title: No explicit bidirectional P2P session prime before coop encounter start
issues.description: Per gotchas doc 2026-04-22: "Sending only from one direction does NOT establish bidirectional sessions" under `ISteamNetworkingMessages`. Lobby create/join paths call `primeP2PSessions(lobbyId)` (see `multiplayerLobbyService.ts:507-513`) but the coop encounter-start path in `encounterBridge.ts:961-978` does NOT re-prime. If Steam closed idle sessions between lobby-ready and encounter-start (minutes of deck selection), the first `mp:coop:enemy_state` send from host may hit a half-open session and fail silently. `_sendWithRetry` helps but adds latency; session may still fail if both sides are out of sync on session state.
issues.fixHypothesis: Before `broadcastSharedEnemyState` (host) and `awaitCoopEnemyReconcile` (guest), both sides call `primeP2PSessions(lobbyId)` and `await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 100))` to let zero-byte primers cross. Log prime result counts.

issues.title: Race progress broadcast fires in coop mode — mode-bleed overwrites partner HUD state
issues.description: Because `activeRunMode === "multiplayer_race"` is unconditionally set for all MP modes (see companion issue), `startRaceProgressBroadcast` is invoked for coop too (`gameFlowController.ts:1099`). It sends `mp:race:progress` every 2 seconds with HP/floor/score. Guest's CardApp then routes this into `opponentProgress` (`CardApp.svelte:861-862`). Meanwhile `onPartnerStateUpdate` (CardApp.svelte:873) ALSO writes into `opponentProgress.playerHp`. Last-write-wins. Visually: race HUD appears where coop HUD should be, and HP flickers between the two sources. This is a large part of the subjective "each in their own game" feel.
issues.fixHypothesis: Guard the race-progress broadcast with `if (activeRunMode === "multiplayer_race" && multiplayerModeState === "race")`. Similarly guard `CardApp.svelte:861-862`.

issues.title: Partner state listener in CardApp overwrites race progress without mode guard
issues.description: `CardApp.svelte:873-890` in `onPartnerStateUpdate`: unconditionally updates `opponentProgress.playerHp` from partner state. Fires in ALL multiplayer modes. In race mode (where no real partner state exists) it's a dead update; in coop mode it fights with race-progress broadcasts for the same `opponentProgress` slot. Either dead code or mode-conflict, depending on mode.
issues.fixHypothesis: Early-return unless `run.multiplayerMode === "coop"`. Or split partner-state and race-progress into separate stores.

issues.title: Orphan UI components: DuelOpponentPanel, RaceResultsScreen, TriviaRoundScreen — built but unrouted
issues.description: Three multiplayer-mode UI components exist and are marked DONE in MP SKILL.md, but are not rendered from any screen router: `DuelOpponentPanel.svelte`, `RaceResultsScreen.svelte`, `TriviaRoundScreen.svelte`. MP SKILL.md "Remaining Work" table acknowledges the first two ("not wired"), but the skill's Phase table still shows them as "DONE (not wired)" which masks the problem. Race mode end-of-run goes to the normal run-end screen, not `RaceResultsScreen`. This contributes to "each in their own game" because there's no explicit "vs opponent" conclusion screen.
issues.fixHypothesis: Either wire each into `CardApp.svelte` routing (add a `raceEnd`/`triviaRound`/`duelCombat` screen and `transitionScreen` invocations), or delete them until the mode is fully implemented. Don't leave "DONE (not wired)" in the skill doc.
issues.resolution: All three components confirmed wired. L-028 stub props replaced with live service state.

issues.title: `mp:lobby:start` single-shot broadcast with no retry on ACK failure
issues.description: `multiplayerLobbyService.ts:1215` broadcasts `mp:lobby:start` once. The retry loop at line 1230-1231 only re-broadcasts if `_pendingStartAcks.size > 0` (guest hasn't ACKed yet). If the guest ACKs "spuriously" (e.g., network blip causes transport to echo a stale ACK) but the start message itself was lost, host considers the guest started and both diverge from that point. Compounded on Steam P2P where server cannot buffer in-flight messages. `contentSelection` is in the start payload (good — avoids race with `mp:lobby:settings`), but if THIS message is lost, the guest may fall back to an older lobby state.
issues.fixHypothesis: Require an ACK that includes a content-selection hash. Host re-broadcasts until ACK carries matching hash. Already partially there — extend the ACK schema.

issues.title: Server `mp:lobby:start` payload spread allows client to inject mode/houseRules/contentSelection into broadcast
issues.description: server/src/routes/mpLobbyWs.ts:266 broadcasts `payload: { ...(msg.payload ?? {}), lobbyId, seed: ... }`. The spread of `msg.payload` forwards EVERY client-supplied key to all guests, with only `lobbyId` and `seed` overwritten. A malicious or buggy host can inject mode override, alternative deckId, fake contentSelection, fake houseRules, or arbitrary new keys. Guests blindly apply to `_currentLobby` because the `mp:lobby:start` client handler trusts payload (per the FIX 020 comment that intentionally widens the spread). FIX 020 solved a real problem — but the right shape is allowlist, not allow-all.
issues.fixHypothesis: Replace the spread with explicit field allowlist: `payload: { lobbyId, seed: msg.payload?.seed ?? null, mode: lobby.mode, houseRules: lobby.houseRules, deckId: lobby.deckId, contentSelection: lobby.contentSelection }` — pull from server-side lobby state, not client input. Drop unknown keys. Add unit test for injection attempts.

issues.title: Duel `mp:duel:cards_played` handler does not validate `action.playerId === msg.senderId` — opponent can credit damage to wrong player
issues.description: multiplayerGameService.ts:1043-1050 stores `_duelState.opponentAction = action` straight from `raw` payload (after value clamping). The handler never compares `raw.playerId` to the transport-layer `msg.senderId`. A peer can submit a DuelTurnAction claiming any playerId. hostResolveTurn() then writes `_duelState.totalDamage[p1Action.playerId] += p1Action.damageDealt` (lines 815-816), so a malicious peer credits damage to/against any chosen player. In ranked Duel this is direct ELO manipulation. Note: only host runs hostResolveTurn, so the host is the gatekeeper that needs the senderId check.
issues.fixHypothesis: In the `mp:duel:cards_played` handler, add `if (action.playerId !== msg.senderId) { console.warn(...); return; }` before storing. Same pattern for any other duel/coop message that claims an identity. Add a property-based test that fuzzes senderId vs payload-playerId mismatch.

issues.title: Duel cards_played arriving before turn_start drops silently — host then waits forever for that player's turn
issues.description: multiplayerGameService.ts ~1013-1024: non-host clients listen to `mp:duel:turn_start` to set `_duelState.turnNumber = payload.turnNumber - 1`. But if Steam P2P reorders messages and `mp:duel:cards_played` arrives BEFORE the turn_start (or before the local _duelState exists at all), the handler hits `if (!_duelState) return` and silently drops the message. No retry, no warning, no resync request. Host then waits indefinitely for the missing card-played from that player and the duel hangs. Steam P2P reordering is documented behavior under packet loss + reliable retry.
issues.fixHypothesis: Add a sequenced bounded buffer: if message arrives with turnNumber > _duelState.turnNumber+1 OR _duelState is null, queue payload (max 4 entries, 5s TTL) and replay after the next turn_start. Log the reorder for diagnostics. Add an out-of-band `mp:duel:resync_request` host can send if it doesn't see opponent action within 8s.

issues.title: Host changing lobby visibility public→password/friends-only mid-session does not evict existing public guests
issues.description: multiplayerLobbyService.setVisibility() updates the `visibility` field and broadcasts new lobby settings, but does not enforce the new constraint on already-joined guests. Guests who joined while the lobby was public stay in a now-private lobby and the host has no way to selectively kick them via the visibility change. Worse, the lobby-browser list will hide the lobby (because filters now exclude it), giving the host a false sense of "private now," but the public-era guests remain. This is also surprising for streamers expecting privacy on toggle.
issues.fixHypothesis: When setVisibility() transitions to a stricter visibility (public→password/friends_only), iterate `_currentLobby.players`, identify any not meeting the new criteria (no friend relationship; no password-knowledge), and call kickPlayer for each with reason 'visibility_changed'. Surface a confirmation modal in the host UI before applying the change.

issues.title: Lobby browser join: clicked entry may be deleted server-side — no preflight existence check, no UX for "lobby disappeared"
issues.description: LobbyBrowserScreen.svelte: list refreshes every 5s. Between a refresh and a user click, the host can leave (deleting the lobby in mpLobbyRegistry) or the lobby can be GC'd by stale TTL. The click invokes joinLobbyById(lobbyId) which hits a 404/empty backend response. The error path surfaces a generic "join failed" toast without distinguishing "lobby disappeared" from "wrong password" or "transport error." Players see noise; debugging is hard. A preflight HEAD/lookup or richer error code would let the UI say "this lobby just closed, refreshing..."
issues.fixHypothesis: Server returns a typed error code `LOBBY_GONE` (mpLobbyRegistry.joinLobby when lobby missing). Client maps to a friendly toast "This lobby just closed" and auto-refreshes the list. Add a brief shake animation on the now-missing row before it disappears.
issues.note: [2026-04-23] [ui-cleanup-wave-3 fix] Added classifyJoinError() helper in LobbyBrowserScreen.svelte that maps lobby-gone error substrings to "That lobby just closed. Refreshing the list." Catch branch now calls void refresh() to reconcile the list, and closes passwordModalEntry when a ghost-lobby is detected. Raw error message preserved for transport-diagnostic cases.

issues.title: Guest who readies up locks the UI awaiting host start — if host's `mp:lobby:start` is lost there is no client-side timeout
issues.description: multiplayerLobbyService.ts ~1200-1250: after setReady(true), the guest UI sets `readyPending=true` and waits for onGameStart to fire. If the host's mp:lobby:start broadcast is dropped (Steam P2P single-shot, or guest disconnects between ACK and broadcast), onGameStart never fires. Guest is stuck: Ready button disabled (MultiplayerLobby.svelte ~438 `disabled={readyPending}`), no error toast, no timeout to release the lock. Player must hard-kill the app or leaveLobby manually. Pairs with existing leaderboard issue MP-STEAM-AUDIT-2026-04-22-H-016 (`mp:lobby:start` no retry) but this one is the UX symptom, not the network fix.
issues.fixHypothesis: After setReady(true) succeeds, start a 30s watchdog. On expiry, surface dismissible error "Host did not start in time" + reset readyPending=false + offer "Leave lobby" or "Try again." Track watchdog id and clear on real onGameStart. Independent of H-016 retry fix.
issues.fixNote: [2026-04-23] UI layer wired: onReadyTimeout subscription in MultiplayerLobby.svelte resets readyPending, shows dismissible error banner ("Host did not start the match. You can ready up again or leave.") with Leave/Try-again actions. Live countdown (1s poll via getReadyWatchdogStatus) shown while waiting. cancelReadyWatchdog() called on dismiss and on game start.

issues.title: Transport singleton survives mode switch solo→MP→solo without explicit destroy — sends to stale lobby endpoint
issues.description: multiplayerTransport.ts:1257-1284: `let _transport: MultiplayerTransport | null = null` lazily created in getMultiplayerTransport() and only nulled in destroyMultiplayerTransport(). Cleanup depends on caller discipline (leaveLobby invokes it). User flows that bypass leaveLobby — browser back button, hard hub-navigation hotkey, error recovery, hub-ENTER while still nominally in a lobby — leave _transport pointing at the old WebSocket/Steam P2P session. Next getMultiplayerTransport() returns the stale instance. Subsequent send() calls route to the old endpoint; new lobby never gets the start broadcast. Particularly nasty when player rejoins a different lobby quickly.
issues.fixHypothesis: Idempotent guard at top of getMultiplayerTransport(mode): if _transport exists and its mode/endpoint differs from the requested mode, destroyMultiplayerTransport() first, then re-create. Also auto-destroy on `hub:enter` event from gameFlowController. Add unit test simulating mode switch round-trip.

issues.title: Server allows same playerId to join lobby twice — second join overwrites first connection record without close
issues.description: server/src/services/mpLobbyRegistry.ts:275: `lobby.connections.set(playerId, conn)` is unconditional. If Player A joins (assigned conn1, joinToken1), then before WS upgrade rejoins (assigned conn2, joinToken2), conn1 is silently replaced. The first ws upgrade later attempts attachWebSocket with joinToken1 — finds no match, rejected. But the underlying conn1.ws (if already attached) is never explicitly closed; orphaned socket leaks. Worst case under tight timing both upgrades succeed but one race wins on broadcast routing. Same issue applies on transient reconnect — old WS handle on conn never gets .close() before being replaced (mpLobbyRegistry.ts:461 attachWebSocket).
issues.fixHypothesis: In joinLobby(): if connections.has(playerId), force-close the previous conn.ws (if any) and emit `mp:lobby:player_replaced` notice. In attachWebSocket(): same — close existing conn.ws before assigning new one. Add tests for double-join + reconnect-while-connected.

issues.title: Unit tests for mp:lobby:start use seed-only stubs
issues.description: src/services/multiplayerLobbyService.test.ts:287, :300 — simulateReceive('mp:lobby:start', { seed: 99999 }) with no mode/deckId/houseRules/contentSelection — Regression coverage cannot catch BUG-1 because no test exercises the full payload. A future refactor could silently reintroduce the same class of bug.
issues.fixHypothesis: Add a test that receives a full mp:lobby:start payload and asserts _currentLobby.mode/deckId/houseRules/contentSelection/seed all match the payload afterward.

issues.title: P2P send/recv channel is 0 by default-argument convention, not a shared constant
issues.description: src/services/steamNetworkingService.ts:654 (send) and :731 (poll startMessagePollLoop default channel = 0) — Future refactor could add an explicit channel on one call site and not the other — silent mismatch, complete P2P blackout with no error.
issues.fixHypothesis: Extract `const P2P_CHANNEL = 0` at module scope and pass explicitly at every call site.
issues.note: MP_P2P_CHANNEL constant added to steamNetworkingService.ts; all send/recv/poll sites use it.

issues.title: session_request_callback handle stored as Some(()) instead of CallbackHandle
issues.description: src-tauri/src/steam.rs:86 declares `_session_request_callback: Option<()>`; :379 assigns Some(()) after registering via networking_messages().session_request_callback(...) — In steamworks-rs, dropping a callback handle de-registers the callback. Storing () instead of the actual handle means the closure may stop firing after the registration call returns — silent session-accept regression.
issues.fixHypothesis: Verify the steamworks-rs 0.12 return type of session_request_callback(); store the actual CallbackHandle wrapped in a Sendable wrapper like _overlay_callback at :87.
issues.note: Documented upstream steamworks-rs 0.12 limitation: session_request_callback discards its CallbackHandle, so callback never fires. Workaround: LobbyChatUpdate + zero-byte send covers all realistic session-acceptance paths. No code change possible until upstream fixes the API.

issues.title: BUG-24 pattern — no general 'ignore events from prior lobby' guard
issues.description: Commits df09f5857 (BUG-24), d15ed5471 each patched one individual path for stale lobby-chat events — Stale events from a prior lobby keep bleeding in through newly exercised code paths. Each fix is one-off; the next MP feature will hit the same bug class.
issues.fixHypothesis: At the callback-dispatch layer in steam.rs / steamNetworkingService.ts, filter every lobby-scoped event against `state.current_lobby_id`. Drop events whose lobby_id doesn't match. Centralised enforcement.
issues.note: LobbyChatUpdate active_lobby_id guard now wraps ALL arms (Entered, Left, Disconnected, Kicked, Banned) in steam.rs. Stale lobby events no longer leak into current sessions.

issues.title: broadcastPartnerState/broadcastSharedEnemyState silently drop when called before initCoopSync
issues.description: src/services/multiplayerCoopSync.ts:489, :511, :528, :629 — 'dropped' warnings without caller being notified — Race between encounter setup and coop init that's supposed to be impossible-by-construction. In practice BUG-6 makes it possible. These logs are the breadcrumbs.
issues.fixHypothesis: Change the silent drop to a buffer-and-flush pattern (like _preSendBuffer in the transport). When initCoopSync runs, flush any queued broadcasts.
issues.note: Pre-init broadcast buffer added to multiplayerCoopSync.ts; broadcastPartnerState/broadcastSharedEnemyState/broadcastEnemyHpUpdate defer-and-flush instead of silently dropping before initCoopSync.

issues.title: _currentLobby mutated in-place — Svelte reactivity may not invalidate watchers
issues.description: src/services/multiplayerLobbyService.ts:1570-1574 — direct assignment of contentSelection/seed/status on the existing object — Svelte $derived watchers keyed on the lobby reference won't re-run when the same object is mutated in place. Commit 1223272ec added a spread workaround for one site but in-place mutation is still the default pattern.
issues.fixHypothesis: Consistently replace _currentLobby with a new object on every mutation (spread + overwrite). Or expose a Svelte store and rely on .set() / .update() instead of mutating the module-local ref.
issues.note: [2026-04-23] [2026-04-23 verify] NOT FIXED by 0c2de9290 — _currentLobby still mutated in-place at 15+ sites; Red-zone deferral

issues.title: Coop on Steam build: each player lands in isolated game state after lobby start (user-reported, primary)
issues.description: Two Steam/Tauri desktop clients can create and join the same coop lobby, confirm both players in the chat roster, and host can press Start Game without error. Both clients transition to the in-run screens. However neither sees the other's damage, HP, block, or turn-end signals — each experiences what looks like a solo run against an enemy that happens to roll identically (because multiplayerSeed is shared) but whose HP diverges from the partner's view. The underlying Steam P2P session that successfully carried lobby chat is, by the time the first mp:coop:enemy_state is broadcast, either (a) not bidirectionally primed in the reverse direction, (b) has a subscription-timing race where the guest's initCoopSync transport.on handlers register AFTER the host's first snapshot send, or (c) silently dropping resolved-false sends past the 3-attempt retry budget fixed in BUG 25. Symptom matches the failure mode of EVERY mp:coop:* message being lost post-handshake — the only messages known to land reliably are those that were part of the lobby chat path before the transition. Works in local-dev two-browser mode because BroadcastChannelTransport and WebSocketTransport don't have the same session-priming problem as SteamP2PTransport. This is the primary bug the user reported; all other issues in this batch are either contributing factors, adjacent risks surfaced during the audit, or hardening work the audit revealed has never been done.

bestEvidence.initialStateBroadcast: src/services/multiplayerCoopSync.ts:516 (transport.send('mp:coop:enemy_state')) — fire-and-forget, no ACK, no resend-on-request
bestEvidence.initCoopSyncWiring: src/CardApp.svelte:~840-860 (registerGameStartCb → startNewRun → initCoopSync) — guest's initCoopSync fires in response to mp:lobby:start handler, which is the same code path host uses to broadcast initial enemy state microseconds later
bestEvidence.recentFixes: BUG 22-27 all touched MP callbacks, lobby-event ordering, P2P session priming, and screen-allowlist. None of them ship with an end-to-end test under SteamP2PTransport, so a gap in the sequence can regress invisibly
bestEvidence.localVsSteam: BroadcastChannelTransport / WebSocketTransport deliver synchronously or through a reliable ordered TCP socket. SteamP2PTransport goes through Tauri IPC → Steamworks ISteamNetworkingMessages → NAT-punched UDP with connection priming — orders of magnitude more state

reproductionSteps: Install Recall Rogue from Steam on two machines signed into different Steam accounts
Host: New Game → Multiplayer → Create Lobby → Coop mode → pick any study-multi deck
Guest: Join Lobby (via Friends list or password browser)
Confirm both players visible in lobby roster, chat works bidirectionally
Both players Ready, host clicks Start Game
Both clients transition to runPreview → dungeonMap → combat
Play any card on host — observe damage applies only to host's local enemy copy
Guest sees same enemy sprite/HP-max (shared seed) but full HP, no sync of host's damage
Either player checks window.__rrMpDebug() — expect stale or empty sharedEnemySubs / partnerStates
issues.suggestedInvestigation: 1. Confirm Steam P2P session bidirectionality BEFORE transitioning to run: add a ping/pong round-trip gate in mp:lobby:start handshake. 2. Make mp:coop:enemy_state ACK-tracked with resend-on-request (guest emits mp:coop:request_initial if no snapshot in 2s). 3. Subscribe-before-start: move initCoopSync(transport.on(...)) registration to fire BEFORE the mp:lobby:start ACK is sent, so the guest is ready for the first enemy_state before host can possibly send it. 4. Add application-layer send-receipt logging that surfaces in __rrMpDebug to tell host whether each message was acknowledged.

issues.title: Coop runs spin up the race-progress broadcast loop — mp:race:progress spam during coop pollutes FSRS analytics and causes premature race-result emission
issues.description: src/services/gameFlowController.ts:437-441 sets activeRunMode = 'multiplayer_race' regardless of whether the lobby mode is coop, duel, or trivia. The gate at line 1094 fires startRaceProgressBroadcast for every MP mode, spamming mp:race:progress every 2s during coop. Coop sync ignores those messages, but race FSRS batch state accumulates and writes at 'race finish'. Coop runs that complete will inadvertently emit race results, polluting analytics and FSRS scheduling history. Distinct from AUDIT-C-003 which covers the activeRunMode bug root; this catalogs the analytics-pollution side-effect.

bestEvidence.analyticsEffect: race FSRS batch accumulates during coop run; emits at 'race finish' corrupting learning data

issues.suggestedFix: Introduce 'multiplayer_coop', 'multiplayer_duel', 'multiplayer_trivia' activeRunMode values. Gate race broadcast strictly on activeRunMode === 'multiplayer_race'.

issues.title: mp:lobby:start retry loop re-fires _gameStartSubscribers on guest mid-init — tears down coop sync on second delivery
issues.description: The host retry fires every 750ms (multiplayerLobbyService.ts:1225-1232). The receiver at line 1560 re-applies seed and re-fires subscribers on each delivery. If the guest's first ACK is dropped (BUG 25 history shows this happens), the second mp:lobby:start arrives mid-startNewRun. startNewRun resets pendingDomainSelection, recreates run state, and re-calls initCoopSync(localPlayerId) — which calls destroyCoopSync() first, wiping any in-flight barrier. Torn-down coop sync mid-init produces exactly the 'each in own game' symptom.

bestEvidence.receiverFires: line 1560 re-fires all _gameStartSubscribers on each delivery
bestEvidence.tearDown: initCoopSync calls destroyCoopSync() before re-initializing, wiping in-flight barrier

issues.suggestedFix: Add idempotency guard: if _currentLobby.status === 'in_game' on the guest when a second mp:lobby:start arrives, just re-send the ACK and return without re-firing subscribers.

issues.title: Player ID identity model mixes three schemes — pending_peer_left matcher cannot find player after settings round-trip
issues.description: Three player ID formats coexist: 'player_<base36>_<rand>' (CardApp.svelte:567 random), 'steam:<hostSteamId>' placeholder (multiplayerLobbyService.ts:587-598/696-708), and persona name. After mp:lobby:join, the host's LobbyPlayer.id for the guest is the random player_xxx. The peer-left poll at line 1668-1685 matches p.id === steamId || p.id === `steam:${steamId}` — neither side of the OR matches the random ID. An ungraceful peer crash leaves a ghost player permanently in roster; allReady() may still pass; host clicks Start; the 3s ACK timeout fires and host enters coop alone.

bestEvidence.peerLeftMatcher: src/services/multiplayerLobbyService.ts:1668-1685 — OR condition never matches random player_xxx ID

issues.suggestedFix: Maintain a playerId→steamId map populated from mp:lobby:join and Steam owner reads. OR make localPlayerId the Steam ID at CardApp.svelte:567 when on Steam, eliminating the steam: placeholder entirely.
issues.note: [2026-04-23] steam: placeholder removed; peer-left matcher still mismatches player_xxx IDs — ghost-player leak persists

issues.title: mp:lobby:settings Object.assign(_currentLobby, settings) clobbers locally-mutated player data on every rebroadcast
issues.description: multiplayerLobbyService.ts:1535 does Object.assign(_currentLobby, settings). Only isReady/readyVersion for the LOCAL player are preserved (lines 1519-1556). Other players' connectionState, multiplayerRating, etc. are lost. A reconnect-marked partner gets re-marked 'connected' on the next settings rebroadcast, even if their P2P session is still broken — making H10's 60s grace timer unreliable. Also: kicks before settings arrive are rejected as spoofed (M3) because guest's hostId is still 'steam:<HOSTSID>' while issuedBy is the host's player_xxx.
issues.suggestedFix: Object.assign EXCEPT preserve locally-known per-player connectionState. Add a settings sequence number and reject stale deliveries (incoming.seq < currentSeq).
issues.note: [2026-04-23] localStateMap snapshot preserves isReady/connectionState/mpRating across Object.assign

issues.title: handleCoopPlayerDeath not called from encounterBridge.applyDamageToLocalPlayer — partner barrier hangs 45s on player death instead of resolving immediately
issues.description: multiplayerCoopSync.ts:24 has a TODO(H18-wiring) comment. handleCoopPlayerDeath is defined at line 621-641 but the call site in encounterBridge.ts where local player HP commits to <=0 never invokes it. When a coop player dies, the partner's barrier hangs on the 45s timeout instead of immediately ending the encounter for both. Prior batch issue 015 covers the TODO existence; this documents the runtime consequence: the 45s wait in combat before recovery, which makes coop feel broken even when sync is otherwise working.

bestEvidence.callSiteMissing: src/services/encounterBridge.ts applyDamageToLocalPlayer does not invoke it

issues.suggestedFix: Call handleCoopPlayerDeath(localPlayerId) wherever local player HP commits to <=0 in coop mode in encounterBridge.ts. Partner subscribes to mp:coop:player_died to break out of barrier immediately.

issues.title: _sendWithRetry exhausts 3 attempts silently — no transport-state transition, no onLobbyError, no UI surface
issues.description: src/services/multiplayerTransport.ts:723-760: when _sendWithRetry exhausts its 3-attempt budget, it logs a diagnostic line and returns silently. No transport-state transition to 'error' fires. No onLobbyError callback is invoked. The host's H5 ACK retry at line 1225 re-broadcasts every 750ms which masks single-message loss, but only while host is in the startGame code path — once host navigates to combat, no retry fires and the guest's ACK never arrives. This is distinct from issue 005 (retry budget too short); this is about the missing failure-escalation path after exhaustion.

bestEvidence.missingBehavior: no transport state transition to 'error', no onLobbyError call, no UI surface on exhaustion

issues.suggestedFix: On retry exhaustion, transition transport to 'error' state and fire onLobbyError. Surface a UI banner so players know the connection is degraded rather than experiencing silent desync.

issues.title: Steam P2P channel 0 cross-lobby contamination — lingering messages from prior lobby may dispatch into new lobby's listeners
issues.description: src/services/steamNetworkingService.ts:651,668: if a player rapidly leaves lobby A and joins lobby B, lingering channel-0 messages from lobby A may still be in the Steam queue. These dispatch to lobby B's listeners. Most are dropped by senderId mismatch, but a stray matching senderId could cause cross-lobby data leaks (stale enemy state, stale turn deltas). No lobbyId field exists in message envelopes to enable filtering.

bestEvidence.mechanism: channel-0 queue not flushed on leaveSteamLobby; no lobbyId envelope field for cross-lobby filtering

issues.suggestedFix: Include lobbyId in every message envelope. Discard messages whose lobbyId doesn't match _activeLobbyId. Flush channel-0 queue after leaveSteamLobby.
issues.note: [2026-04-23] Envelope + recv filter built, but setActiveLobby() not called from normal join paths — filter INERT in production | [2026-04-23 wiring] setActiveLobby() now called after connect() in createLobby (both deferred/non-deferred), joinLobby, joinLobbyById, and null-cleared in leaveLobby — 5 callers total (was 1). Cross-lobby Channel 0 filter now armed in production.

issues.title: MP-006 (leave lobby softlock) and MP-007 (race enterRoom black screen) from MP-20260413-003941 batch remain unaddressed across all 27 BUG fixes

Both are showstoppers for any race mode playtest.

bestEvidence.bugCoverage: BUG 27 (61a60edd9) patched gameScreens allowlist but not the leave or enterRoom paths
