issues.title: transport.connect() called AFTER first transport.send() in joinLobby paths — first message races against primeP2PSessions and may be silently dropped
issues.description: In multiplayerLobbyService.ts:639,646,651,755, transport.connect() is called after the first transport.send() is already queued. If _sendWithRetry exhausts the 3-attempt cap before primeP2PSessions completes, the first message (typically the critical mp:lobby:join or settings bootstrap) is silently lost. docs/architecture/multiplayer.md:639 claims primeP2PSessions is 'called in all 3 lobby join/create paths' — true, but always AFTER the first send is queued.

bestEvidence.docClaim: docs/architecture/multiplayer.md:639 — 'primeP2PSessions called in all 3 paths' (true but fire-and-forget, races first send)

issues.suggestedFix: Await primeP2PSessions(lobbyId) BEFORE the first transport.send, or lift the initial send into a 'wait for accept then send' wrapper that the priming callback triggers.
issues.note: [2026-04-23] Interface wiring + JSDoc only; primeP2PSessions-before-first-send race not addressed

issues.title: No idempotent guard on mp:lobby:start re-entry — subscriber closures from CardApp re-mounts stack, causing double-fire of onGameStart scene transition
issues.description: multiplayerLobbyService.ts:864-865 intentionally does not clear subscribers in leaveLobby (existing design). CardApp re-mount via HMR, error-boundary recovery, or devtools refresh leaves old subscriber closures registered. New closures stack on top. A double-fire of onGameStart re-triggers scene transition mid-run. The unsubscribe returned by onGameStart is never stored or called in CardApp's $effect cleanup.

bestEvidence.cardAppGap: src/CardApp.svelte $effect cleanup does not call the unsubscribe returned by onGameStart

issues.suggestedFix: In CardApp's $effect cleanup, explicitly call the unsubscribe function returned by onGameStart to prevent stale closure accumulation.
issues.note: [2026-04-23] JSDoc only; CardApp already cleaned up correctly; no structural dedup guard in service

issues.title: No lint guards against bool-vs-void IPC contract erasure — same class hit 3 times across BUGs 1, 2, 25
issues.description: The pattern of Promise<boolean> returns where the caller only .catch()es without inspecting the resolved value has caused at least 3 silent failures across BUGs 1, 2, and 25 in the multiplayer stack. steamNetworkingService.ts typed map and multiple sites in multiplayerTransport.ts contain .catch() handlers that swallow the boolean result. No lint exists to detect new instances. The same class of bug will recur when new IPC methods are added.

bestEvidence.pattern: Promise<boolean> return where caller .catch()es without checking resolved value

issues.suggestedFix: Ship scripts/lint/check-bool-promise-erasure.mjs that scans for Promise<boolean> returns where the caller only .catch()es and never inspects the resolved value. Run in pre-commit hook.

issues.title: Catch-up mastery assignment uses unseeded `Math.random()` — card power diverges on acquisition
issues.description: `catchUpMasteryService.ts:40` uses `0.5 + Math.random()` to set starting mastery level for newly acquired cards. In coop, both clients acquire the same card but with different mastery → different damage + charge values for that card from then on. Not always visible (some cards no-op mastery) but affects any mastery-scaling card.
issues.fixHypothesis: Use `getRunRng("catchUpMastery").next()` in place of `Math.random()`.

issues.title: Shop food elixir/feast (floor 10+) roll uses unseeded `Math.random()`
issues.description: `shopService.ts:237`: `Math.random() < 0.30` decides elixir vs feast in late-floor shop. Different result on host vs guest. Cosmetic if both players roll once, but confusing if one sees "elixir" and other sees "feast" in same shop.
issues.fixHypothesis: Seed with `getRunRng("shopInventory").next()`.

issues.title: Quiz question type rollup uses unseeded `Math.random()`
issues.description: Approx `src/services/nonCombatQuizSelector.ts:~290` (exact line per Agent D report). Different question framings (identify vs reverse vs continent) on each client. Not a correctness issue but a UX-coherence issue ("why am I seeing a different question type than my partner?").
issues.fixHypothesis: Use `getRunRng("quizFraming").next()` — new fork label.

issues.title: `session_request_callback.accept()` return value logged but not checked
issues.description: `src-tauri/src/steam.rs:308-313`: the callback logs `accepted` boolean but never branches on false. If Steam rejects the session-accept (rare but possible under quota/NAT conditions), we continue as if accepted. Subsequent messages from that peer fail with ConnectFailed and the retry path masks the original failure.
issues.fixHypothesis: On `accepted == false`: log WARN, stash error into `last_session_errors` map, try a zero-byte RELIABLE send to force a retry (matches LobbyChatUpdate auto-accept pattern).
issues.note: TS side: getPendingSessionRequestError() getter added to steamNetworkingService.ts (polls steam_get_pending_session_request_error). Rust side: pending_session_request_error slot + command needed in steam.rs. UI surface pending per PASS1-BUG-21. | [2026-04-23] accept() return stored + getter exported; but callback may never fire due to 059 — dead at runtime

issues.title: `steam_request_lobby_data` is a silent no-op on steamworks-rs 0.12
issues.description: `src-tauri/src/steam.rs:1082-1093`: function always returns `Ok(false)` due to crate API gap. TS side relies on a 200ms sleep workaround (wave 12). Lobby metadata (mode, title, lobby_code) arrives eventually via `LobbyDataUpdate_t` but first read may get empty strings — this has caused UI to show placeholder mode until warm-up completes.
issues.fixHypothesis: Options: (a) upgrade steamworks-rs to 0.13+ if method exposed, (b) add `steamworks-sys` direct dep + call flat C API (`SteamAPI_ISteamMatchmaking_RequestLobbyData`), (c) leave documented workaround as-is. (c) is acceptable for now.
issues.note: Verified no-op by design: steam_request_lobby_data is a documented steamworks-rs 0.12 API gap. The 200ms warm-up sleep in joinSteamLobby covers the callback-pump window. No code change needed.

issues.title: Fastify web backend removed but `webBackend` / `pickBackend` still picks it as fallback on Steam builds
issues.description: In `multiplayerLobbyService.ts` the `pickBackend()` cascade selects between steamBackend, webBackend, broadcastBackend. On a Tauri Steam build with Steam init OK, the expected path is steamBackend. But if Steam init partially fails (Steam offline / appid missing in a dev iteration), the code falls back to webBackend, which tries to hit `VITE_MP_WS_URL`. If the production build was not compiled with that env var, webBackend silently fails. User perception: "lobby works but nothing works after start". Inspect actual cascade order and ensure Steam failure does NOT demote to web in production.
issues.fixHypothesis: Audit `pickBackend()`; in Steam release, hard-fail instead of falling back to web. Log which backend was picked on every lobby create/join.
issues.note: [2026-04-23] pickBackend() throws when isDesktop=true and isTauriPresent()=false

issues.title: LAN tab hidden but fallback paths still compiled — `lanServerService` starts + stops on lobby create
issues.description: Per commit message 7a968aae5, LAN server is auto-stopped on Steam lobby create/join. But the LAN-related reconnect timers still run. In release Steam builds the LAN code is dead weight at best; at worst an errant `isLanMode()` true could pin webBackend. Verify LAN tab hide is sufficient (see commit f464d3652).
issues.fixHypothesis: Compile LAN out of Steam release via `import.meta.env.VITE_STEAM_BUILD` check, or at minimum assert `isLanMode() === false` at lobby create in Steam builds.
issues.note: [2026-04-23] LAN_DISABLED_IN_STEAM gates all LAN services; ?lan=1 escape hatch

issues.title: No `__rrMpDebug()` snapshot captured in user-submitted bug reports — diagnosis is blind
issues.description: Commit BUG20 (`a18cdb851` area) added `window.__rrMpDebug()` for snapshotting MP state. There is no in-game UI button or automatic-on-bug trigger to surface this for players. Reports like "coop is broken" arrive without a debug dump. For Steam launch this blocks triage.
issues.fixHypothesis: Add a "Copy MP Debug Snapshot" button to the MP lobby menu (visible only when `mpLobbyActive`). Writes __rrMpDebug output to clipboard for pasting into bug reports.

issues.title: `awaitCoopTurnEnd` barrier timeout is 45s — too long for perceived responsiveness
issues.description: `multiplayerCoopSync.ts` BARRIER_TIMEOUT_MS = 45000 per SKILL.md line 37. In practice, if the partner disconnects mid-turn, the surviving player waits 45 seconds before game recovers. Should be lower + paired with faster liveness (ping/pong at 2-5s) so disconnect is caught quickly.
issues.fixHypothesis: Reduce to 15s. Pair with `mp:ping` at 3s interval; if 2 consecutive pings miss, trigger `handleCoopPlayerDeath` path.
issues.note: [2026-04-23] BARRIER_TIMEOUT_MS 45000→15000 across both barrier functions

issues.title: Server lobby code generator uses `Math.random()` with no collision check — birthday-problem at ~1k concurrent lobbies
issues.description: server/src/services/mpLobbyRegistry.ts:149-156: generateLobbyCode() picks 6 chars from a 32-char alphabet (32^6 ≈ 1.07B). Math.random is non-cryptographic and the function does not check `lobbies.has(code)` before returning. Birthday-problem expectation: collisions emerge around √1.07B ≈ 32k lobbies — but with Math.random bias on V8, observable collisions can appear earlier under concurrent generation. On collision, two lobbies share a join code; whichever was inserted last wins on `resolveByCode()` lookup, hijacking the other. Low probability for current player counts but ship-blocker for any tournament/event.
issues.fixHypothesis: Loop until `!lobbies.has(code)`; bound the loop at 16 attempts and bail with INTERNAL_ERROR if exceeded. Switch to crypto.randomBytes(4) → base32-encode → 6-char slice for better distribution. Add unit test seeding two concurrent createLobby() calls with rigged Math.random.

issues.title: Server stores `multiplayerRating` from client join payload and broadcasts to other players — no profile-DB validation
issues.description: server/src/services/mpLobbyRegistry.ts:217, 272: MpLobbyConnection.multiplayerRating is initialized to 1500 if not supplied, BUT the field is read from the client-supplied join payload. A modified client can submit `{ multiplayerRating: 9999 }` and have it rebroadcast in player_joined / lobby_settings. ELO matchmaking on the server uses these values to bracket lobbies. Result: skill-based matchmaking is bypassable from the client — a smurf can claim 800 to find weak opponents, or a low-rated player can claim 2000 for bragging-rights display in lobby UI.
issues.fixHypothesis: On joinLobby, ignore client-supplied multiplayerRating. Look up rating from authoritative source (when persistent profiles ship: profileService; until then: server-side in-memory ELO map keyed by signed playerId). Document that client value is informational only.

issues.title: Lobby join capacity check is non-atomic — two simultaneous joins can both pass `currentPlayers < maxPlayers` and exceed cap
issues.description: server/src/services/mpLobbyRegistry.ts ~310: capacity gate compares `lobby.currentPlayers < lobby.maxPlayers` then increments. Node's single-threaded event loop normally serializes this safely BUT joinLobby is async (awaits hash check, await on player profile lookup). Two concurrent join requests can both pass the read of `currentPlayers` before either commits the increment, then both complete and the lobby holds `maxPlayers + 1`. Most visible at the moment a 4-player lobby fills as two scrambling guests click join. Symptom: lobby UI shows 5/4 slots; mode-min-player gates incorrectly satisfied.
issues.fixHypothesis: Hoist all sync state mutations out of the await boundary: read currentPlayers, do the await, then re-check currentPlayers === pre-await value before committing. OR introduce a per-lobby async lock (simple Promise-chained mutex) around joinLobby. Add concurrent-join unit test.

issues.title: Trivia Night: late-arriving answer flagged timeout via `selectedIndex=-1` but `timingMs` is NOT clamped — UI shows "13.5s" for a "missed" answer
issues.description: triviaNightService.ts:625-640: handler accepts answer arriving after `timeLimitMs + ANSWER_GRACE_MS`, sets `selectedIndex: -1` to mark timeout, but stores `timingMs: rawTimingMs` unchanged. That is broadcast to peers in `mp:trivia:scores` (line 672). Downstream UI (TriviaRoundScreen) and per-question summaries display `timingMs` directly. Players see "Player X timed out — answered in 13.5s" which is internally contradictory. Analytics recording answer latency double-count timeouts.
issues.fixHypothesis: Clamp timingMs alongside selectedIndex: `timingMs: selectedIndex === -1 ? null : rawTimingMs`. Update TriviaAnswer type to allow null. UI: render "timed out" without a timing string when timingMs is null.

issues.title: Trivia Night: clients trust host-computed `allIncorrect` flag without local recompute — silent corruption propagates
issues.description: triviaNightService.ts:454: host computes `allIncorrect = playerAnswers.every(a => !a.correct)` from the answer set, stamps it on the broadcast payload. Non-host clients (line 672 mp:trivia:scores handler) apply the flag to UI directly. If host's answer set was corrupted (race in host-side answer collection, or BUG in the dedupe logic), guests propagate the corruption with no integrity check. The same payload contains playerAnswers — guests can recompute trivially.
issues.fixHypothesis: In the mp:trivia:scores handler, recompute allIncorrect from `result.playerAnswers`. If it diverges from host-supplied flag, log warning + use locally computed value. Same pattern for any host-derived summary that can be reproduced from the same payload.

issues.title: Trivia Night dedupe `_usedFactIds` polluted by stale timer closure when game restarts in rapid succession
issues.description: triviaNightService.ts:144 `_usedFactIds: Set<string>` is module-scoped. Cleared at line 235 (initTriviaGame) and line 527 (destroyTriviaGame). If a player triggers "Play Again" before the previous game's pending `setTimeout` (next-question scheduler) fires, the stale closure in that timeout calls `_usedFactIds.add(factId)` against the new game's freshly cleared set. The new game now has IDs from the previous game pre-loaded into dedupe, biasing fact selection. With small fact pools the bias is large.
issues.fixHypothesis: Make _usedFactIds an instance field of a TriviaSession class instantiated per game; or generation-counter pattern: bump `_gameGen` on each init, capture gen at scheduling time, in the timeout check `if (myGen !== _gameGen) return;`. Add test that schedules a question, then re-inits, and asserts the late callback is a no-op.

issues.title: Race FSRS batch is async and not awaited — concurrent solo run start can read mid-update mastery state
issues.description: multiplayerGameService.ts:281-304 `_applyRaceFsrsBatch()` is async; called fire-and-forget on race finish (lines 207, 575). Each iteration awaits updateReviewState() per fact. Player who clicks "Play Again" / hub fast enough to start a solo run before the batch resolves will trigger encounterBridge.ts updateReviewState() calls on the same fact IDs. Reads of mastery tier (used to power cards in the new run) can land between batch update steps, returning stale tier. Card power ends up wrong for the first encounter of the new run.
issues.fixHypothesis: Race finish path awaits `_applyRaceFsrsBatch()` before transitioning to RaceResultsScreen / hub. OR: gate `startNewRun` until `_lastRaceFsrsBatchPromise` resolves. Surface a "Saving knowledge progress…" spinner during the wait. Add test that interleaves race finish + new-run start and checks mastery consistency.

issues.title: Lobby/game subscribers persist across leave+rejoin — second race start fires onGameStart callbacks twice
issues.description: multiplayerLobbyService.ts ~94-114: `_lobbyUpdateSubscribers` and `_gameStartSubscribers` are intentionally preserved across leave/rejoin (per inline comment ~line 904). UI components register on mount. If user rejoins same/different lobby without component re-mount, old subscribers remain AND new ones get added on re-render of any subscribing component (e.g., MultiplayerLobby.svelte $effect re-run). Second race-start fires every callback twice. Visible symptoms: initRaceMode called twice (resets fact accumulators mid-race), onGameStart screen transition runs twice (double navigation, brief flash).
issues.fixHypothesis: Track subscribers in a Map<componentId, callback> instead of Set; on each register, replace the entry. OR: change UI to always unsubscribe in $effect cleanup. Simpler: on leaveLobby, snapshot subscribers count and warn if > expected baseline.

issues.title: No rate limiting on `POST /mp/lobbies` or `GET /mp/lobbies` — single client can spam-create 1000s of stale lobbies
issues.description: server/src/index.ts:371-373 registers MP routes with no rate limiter. By contrast, `duelRateLimit` and `guildRateLimit` (120/min) gate other endpoints. A bot can POST hundreds of createLobby calls per second; each lobby is 10-minute TTL but that's 600/min × 10 = 6000 lobbies in memory at steady state per attacker, plus each lobby holds a heartbeat timer. Memory + CPU drain. Same for GET /mp/lobbies: each call walks the lobby map and emits JSON; spammable.
issues.fixHypothesis: Apply a per-IP rate limiter: 30/min for POST /mp/lobbies, 60/min for GET. Document the limit in the API contract. For authenticated clients (when auth ships), key off playerId not IP.

issues.title: LAN URL persists in localStorage across sessions — Steam build relaunched without `?lan=1` still routes transport to LAN
issues.description: multiplayerTransport.ts:1250 + lanConfigService.ts: `isLanMode()` reads localStorage on every call. If a session set the LAN URL (via the LAN tab) and the user quits + relaunches Steam build without explicit LAN flag, localStorage still holds the URL, isLanMode() returns true, createTransport() picks WebSocketTransport pointing at the (now-unreachable) LAN URL. The clearLanModeOnHubEntry() helper exists (~line 1441) but only fires on explicit hub navigation, not on app boot. Symptom: Steam P2P silently bypassed; player joins a lobby and no one else can find them.
issues.fixHypothesis: On app boot (CardApp onMount), if Tauri build AND localStorage holds a LAN URL with no live LAN server discovered within 1.5s, clear the LAN URL. Show a one-time toast "Switched to Steam multiplayer" so the user understands the change.

issues.title: LAN discovery toSubnetPrefix() does not strip IPv6 `%scope` suffix — link-local probe URLs are invalid and silently fail
issues.description: lanDiscoveryService.ts:121-145 `toSubnetPrefix()` splits IPv6 on `:` but does not strip `%scope` suffixes (e.g., macOS `fe80::1%en0`). When building probe hosts the full string with scope is passed to probeLanServer() which constructs `http://[fe80::1%en0]/...` — invalid URL (% not percent-encoded, browsers reject scope IDs in HTTP). Probe fails with malformed-URL error; LAN host on link-local IPv6 is undiscoverable.
issues.fixHypothesis: In toSubnetPrefix(), strip `%scope` before parsing: `addr.split('%')[0]`. Reject link-local IPv6 entirely from probe targets (fe80::/10 is non-routable beyond the local segment anyway). Add unit test for `fe80::1%en0` input.

issues.title: startLanServer() exception (port in use, macOS Local Network denied) propagates uncaught — caller has no error UX
issues.description: lanServerService.ts:185-190 wraps tauriInvokeOrThrow() in `Promise.race([invoke, timeout])` but no `.catch()`. On macOS Local Network permission-denied (after the user dismisses the system prompt) or EADDRINUSE on the Rust side, the rejection bubbles up to the caller. The MultiplayerMenu LAN-tab handler currently does NOT have a try/catch around startLanServer(); the unhandled rejection lands as a console error and the LAN button reverts to its default state with no user feedback.
issues.fixHypothesis: Wrap the Promise.race result in try/catch; return `{ ok: false, error: Error.code, hint: "Grant Local Network permission in System Settings → Privacy" }`. MultiplayerMenu surfaces the hint as a dismissible banner. Already partially solved for `macosPermissionHint` (BUG15) but the rejection path is the orthogonal failure.

issues.title: invokeSteam null-coalesces errors — no distinction between unavailable / IPC denied / command threw
issues.description: src/services/steamNetworkingService.ts:99, :117 — Every P2P silent failure logs the same way. No way to tell from logs whether Steam is down, Tauri blocked the command, or the Rust handler errored.
issues.fixHypothesis: Pattern-match errors in invokeSteam: distinguish 'Steam client not running' from 'Tauri IPC rejected' from 'command threw Rust-side'. rrLog each distinctly.
issues.note: sendP2PMessage and acceptP2PSession now log distinct rrLog entries when invokeSteam returns null (IPC failure) vs Rust returning false (session failure). getLastSteamInvokeError() captures the IPC error detail for callers.

issues.title: Rust debug.log path not surfaced in-game
issues.description: src-tauri/src/main.rs:15-38 — platform-specific path, never shown to user — Bug reports from end users arrive without the one log that contains Steam diagnostics. Support cycle gets longer.
issues.fixHypothesis: Show the log path in the Settings → About screen, and in any MP error banner. Copy-to-clipboard button.

issues.title: crit_lens relic uses Math.random() — co-op peers roll independent crits for same card play
issues.description: In relicEffectResolver.ts line 1647, the crit_lens relic's 25% crit chance uses a bare Math.random() call. In co-op mode, both clients independently compute damage for the shared enemy via delta submission. If one client crits and the other doesn't, the two deltas submitted to the host barrier will differ for the same card play. The host merges deltas additively, so the final reconciled enemy HP will be correct, BUT the crit outcome feedback shown to each player will differ — one sees a crit flash, the other doesn't. If the delta computation itself uses crit to modify the damage value that goes into the delta, the two clients will submit different damage values for the same card, leading to inflated or deflated total damage in the merged snapshot.

bestEvidence.coopMechanism: EnemyTurnDelta.damageDealt = preTurnSnapshot.currentHP - enemy.currentHP after all card plays; if crit doubles a card's damage on one client but not the other, the deltas diverge

reproductionSteps: Two co-op peers, both holding crit_lens relic
Either peer plays a Charge card during their turn
Peer A crits (25% chance), Peer B does not
Both submit EnemyTurnDelta to the barrier
Peer A's delta shows higher damageDealt than Peer B's for the same card
Host merges: result is sum of both deltas — enemy takes non-crit damage twice instead of crit + non-crit

issues.title: obsidian_dice relic uses Math.random() — co-op peers independently roll +50%/-25% multiplier
issues.description: In relicEffectResolver.ts line 1712, the obsidian_dice relic applies a 50/50 multiplier roll (+50% or -25%) using Math.random(). Same issue as crit_lens: in co-op, both clients resolve the effect independently against their local enemy copy. If one rolls high and the other rolls low, the damage deltas submitted to the turn-end barrier will disagree for the same card play event.

bestEvidence.coopMechanism: Same delta-submission path as crit_lens issue

reproductionSteps: Two co-op peers, at least one holding obsidian_dice relic
Peer plays a Charge card
One client rolls the +50% branch, other rolls the -25% branch
Deltas diverge — merged result is the arithmetic sum of the two divergent outcomes

issues.title: Guest's initCoopSync() subscribes to mp:coop:enemy_state AFTER host has already broadcast the initial snapshot
issues.description: Audit of CardApp.svelte shows the guest's onGameStart callback fires the full chain `persistPlayer() → startNewRun() → initMapNodeSync() → initCoopSync(localPlayerId)` in response to receiving mp:lobby:start. The HOST starts encounter-zero almost immediately after receiving the guest's ACK and broadcasts the first mp:coop:enemy_state. If the host's send beats the guest's initCoopSync (which registers transport.on('mp:coop:enemy_state') at line 206 of multiplayerCoopSync.ts), the guest NEVER receives the initial snapshot. There is no replay buffer on the transport and no resend-on-request on the host. The guest falls back to its own local enemy copy (generated from the shared seed) which will diverge the moment either player applies damage. This is a textbook subscribe-after-publish race — exact odds depend on host-vs-guest processing speed and Steam P2P latency, but it's not a probabilistic edge case, it's a deterministic outcome under a plausible scheduling order.

bestEvidence.subscriberOwner: src/CardApp.svelte registerGameStartCb → initCoopSync() called inside the guest's lobby-start handler
bestEvidence.publisher: src/services/multiplayerCoopSync.ts:516 transport.send('mp:coop:enemy_state', snapshot) — host emits at encounter-zero setup
bestEvidence.noReplayBuffer: MultiplayerTransport.on() starts empty; messages sent before subscription land in /dev/null
bestEvidence.noResendProtocol: Guest has no way to request missed initial state — silent fallback to local seeded enemy copy

reproductionSteps: Add instrumented timestamps to multiplayerCoopSync.ts send + recv for mp:coop:enemy_state
Start coop session on two Steam clients
Compare host's send timestamp vs guest's subscriber-register timestamp
Expect intermittent ordering where send precedes subscribe by 10-200ms
issues.suggestedFix: Option A: host buffers the most-recent enemy_state snapshot and resends on every new lobby member event. Option B: guest emits mp:coop:request_initial_state on its first run-screen mount; host replies with current snapshot. Option C: move transport.on() registration for mp:coop:* listeners to lobby-join time (before any game-start signal), so they're live as soon as the player is in the lobby.

issues.suggestedFix: Register a Rust-side P2PSessionConnectFail_t callback and bridge to TS via a new pending_p2p_fail slot mirroring the pending_peer_left mechanism.
issues.note: [2026-04-23] Rust callback + getPendingP2PFail() exported but ZERO CALLERS; TODO(H10-transport) persists | [2026-04-23 wiring] initP2PFailPollLoop() running 2s poll from createLobby/joinLobby/joinLobbyById, teardown via _p2pFailPollCleanup. onP2PFail listener registered in setupMessageHandlers — maps peerSteamId → playerId and starts 60s reconnect grace timer. TODO(H10-transport) removed.

issues.title: 'mp:lobby:start_ack' as MultiplayerMessageType cast in lobbyService hides future rename regressions
issues.description: multiplayerLobbyService.ts:1581 casts 'mp:lobby:start_ack' with `as MultiplayerMessageType` suppressing the TypeScript rename-refactor check. If the message type is ever renamed in the union, this cast silently compiles to the old string value while the rest of the codebase uses the new name.
issues.suggestedFix: Drop the cast and use the literal type directly if it is in the union, or add it to the union if it was omitted.
issues.note: [2026-04-23] [2026-04-23 verify] Credit corrected: cast removal landed in 77c71eaca (BUG-1/2/3/4/14), not 8f5d7a74b which added zero bytes for this

issues.title: 'local_player' placeholder risk — if getLocalPlayerId() returns literal 'local_player', both Steam clients consider each other self and route messages to themselves
issues.description: If _localPlayerId is never upgraded from its 'local_player' default (e.g., mp:lobby:join fires before CardApp sets the real ID), any message with senderId === 'local_player' is treated as self-originated and ignored. Both clients dropping each other's messages produces the 'each in own game' symptom. grep confirms 'local_player' literal appears as a default in at least one initialization path.

bestEvidence.risk: both clients ignore each other's messages if senderId matches self-ID placeholder

issues.suggestedFix: Assert at lobby create/join time that _localPlayerId !== 'local_player'. Throw an error if violated. Ensure CardApp sets localPlayerId before any transport send.
issues.note: [2026-04-23] throw on local_player/empty playerId in create/join paths

issues.title: Trivia Night submitAnswer accepts any integer for selectedIndex — out-of-bounds value creates ghost selection in reveal UI
issues.description: triviaNightService.ts:374: submitAnswer(selectedIndex, timingMs) does not validate `selectedIndex ∈ [0, options.length-1]`. A misbehaving client (or fuzzer) sends `selectedIndex: 99`. Host stores it; broadcasts in playerAnswers. TriviaRoundScreen.svelte:152 getOptionRevealClass(index) checks `index === lastRoundResult.correctIndex` and `index === localSelectedIndex`. A selectedIndex of 99 matches no option index, so the reveal UI never highlights it — the player's "selection" silently disappears at reveal time. Confusing UX, low-severity but easy fix.
issues.fixHypothesis: Clamp at submission boundary: `if (selectedIndex < -1 || selectedIndex >= currentQuestion.options.length) selectedIndex = -1` (treat as timeout). Log invalid values for diagnostics.

issues.title: MultiplayerLobby copy-code button stuck "Copied!" after rapid double-click — timeout cleared without resetting state
issues.description: MultiplayerLobby.svelte:263-267 handleCopyCode() guards `if (copyFeedbackTimeout !== null) clearTimeout(copyFeedbackTimeout)` but does not reset `copyFeedback` flag. Rapid double-click: first click sets feedback=true + schedules timeout(2000ms). Second click clears timeout but not feedback. New timeout scheduled; eventually fires. End state: flag stays true longer than expected, button label appears stuck. Compounds with the onDestroy not resetting copyFeedback when the timeout was still scheduled.
issues.fixHypothesis: In handleCopyCode after clearTimeout: `copyFeedbackTimeout = null`. In onDestroy: `copyFeedback = false; copyFeedbackTimeout = null` after clearTimeout. One-line cleanup, ships with no risk.
issues.note: [2026-04-23] [ui-cleanup-wave fix] Applied defensive cleanup to copyFeedbackTimeout handling: null handle between clearTimeout and reassignment; reset copyFeedback=false + handle=null in onDestroy so nothing sticks across hot-reload. MultiplayerLobby.svelte.

issues.title: WebSocket transport reconnect race: onclose+onerror both fire and both can pass the timeout-handle guard
issues.description: multiplayerTransport.ts:486-503 scheduleReconnect() guards `if (reconnectTimeoutHandle !== null) return` then `reconnectAttempts++` then `setTimeout(...)`. If onclose fires (handle null → increments + schedules) and onerror fires synchronously immediately after (handle still null because the set just executed but the closure captured pre-set state — depends on engine), reconnectAttempts can be incremented twice. Cosmetic: at most a wasted attempt slot. Real bug only manifests under tight ordering on slow networks.
issues.fixHypothesis: Add boolean `_reconnectScheduled` flag set true before the increment, false in the timeout callback. Guard on the flag, not the handle. Tiny, stable, no behavior change in the happy path.

issues.title: destroyTriviaGame() never called — module-level state leaks across trivia-night sessions
issues.description: Effect cleanup at CardApp.svelte:1037 calls _pendingTriviaCleanup() (removes transport listeners) but never calls destroyTriviaGame(). The six module-level Maps (_pointTotals, _correctCounts, _timingSums, _answeredCounts, _answers) and all callback refs persist across games. A second trivia night in the same session inherits stale standings, stale answer data, and callbacks pointing at unmounted components.
reproductionSteps: Play a full trivia night → return to lobby → start a second trivia night → first round standings show carry-over scores.

issues.title: Coop rest site actions (heal/study/meditate) are local-only — no barrier or partner notification

issues.title: Floor 4→6 boss encounter is 3.36x harder than preceding floor (all profiles)
issues.description: Across all 6 skill profiles and 28,000 runs, the average turns per encounter jumps from 6.68 on floor 4 to 22.44 on floor 6 — a 3.36x ratio that exceeds the 2.0x spike threshold by 68%. Floor 6 hosts two boss-tier enemies: The Final Exam (27.5 avg turns, 77-97% win rate depending on profile) and The Burning Deadline (23.6 avg turns, 77% win rate for new_player). For weaker profiles the damage spike compounds: language_learner loses 30.7 avg HP on floor 6 encounters vs 6-10 HP on floors 1-4. This is the single largest difficulty cliff in the game.

issues.title: Ascension 15-20 win rates indicate a progression cliff where difficulty outpaces player agency
issues.description: The engagement_low_fun rule fires when regular/gamer proxies fall below too_hard_threshold. At ascension 15, experienced@asc15 drops to 55% (within range), but master@asc15 is 31% — only marginally above the 32.5% too_hard threshold. At asc20 both profiles are catastrophically below: experienced@asc20 at 2.7% and master@asc20 at 9.1%. The progression from asc10 (72%/42%) to asc15 (55%/31%) to asc20 (2.7%/9.1%) shows a non-linear cliff rather than a smooth curve — asc20 isn't 'harder', it's a different game.

issues.title: Chess Tactics: interactive board never renders — fenPosition data mismatch causes silent fallback to text MCQ
issues.description:
The chess_tactics deck has quizResponseMode: 'chess_move', fenPosition, and solutionMoves on every fact. StudyQuizOverlay.svelte DOES import ChessBoard.svelte (line 7) and HAS the chess rendering branch (line 211: 'quizResponseMode === chess_move && chessContext'). The pipeline correctly forwards all chess fields through nonCombatQuizSelector → generateStudyQuestions → QuizQuestion. The board never renders because getPlayerContext() in chessGrader.ts throws when applying solutionMoves[0] to the stored fenPosition.

Root cause: The fenPosition stored in chess_tactics.json facts is the puzzle position AFTER the opponent's setup move (the position the player sees). getPlayerContext() expects the BASE FEN before the setup move and applies solutionMoves[0] to produce the puzzle FEN. Example: fact chess_tac_4172G has fenPosition='1rb3k1/pp1pB1p1/3Pp2p/q1p2n2/2P3Q1/2bB1P2/P1R4P/5KNR b - - 3 20' with solutionMoves=['e4g4','f5e3','f1e2','e3g4']. There is no piece at e4 in this FEN, so applying e4g4 throws. The catch block at StudyQuizOverlay.svelte line 77-79 silently sets chessContext=null. Board branch at line 211 is skipped. Falls back to text MCQ with no error visible to player or in console.

Contrast: chessPuzzleService.ts stores puzzle.fen as the BASE FEN correctly (before setup move). chess_tactics.json was authored with the wrong FEN convention. Also: raw puzzle ID tag '[#Goifm]' is leaked in question text — separate content fix needed.
reproductionSteps: Load __rrScenario.load('study-deck-chess') in Docker container
Wait 7s for scene to init
Observe: 4 multiple-choice buttons with chess notation text (no chess board rendered)
Question shows 'White to move. [#Goifm]' — raw puzzle ID visible in brackets
Note: StudyQuizOverlay.svelte line 7 imports ChessBoard, line 211 has chess branch — the UI code IS present

issues.title: Non-coop multiplayer modes (race, duel, trivia_night, same_cards) untested on Steam build
issues.description: User reported coop is broken on Steam and explicitly noted they have not checked other modes. All modes share the same transport layer, lobby-start handshake, and post-handshake session establishment path. The same subscription-after-send, buffered-send-budget, and initial-state-delivery bugs that affect coop are almost certainly present for race (which depends on mp:race:progress being delivered reliably every ~2s), duel (real-time head-to-head with per-turn messages), same_cards (race + card determinism), and trivia_night (pure quiz coordination). Risk-rank these modes as likely broken until explicitly tested.

bestEvidence.sharedHandshake: src/services/multiplayerLobbyService.ts startGame() — identical H5 ACK flow for all modes

issues.suggestedFix: After fixing the primary coop bug, run each mode end-to-end on two Steam clients and log findings. Add a mode-parameterized version of the proposed integration test (MP-STEAM-20260422-006) that covers all 5 modes' first-message paths.

issues.title: Initial enemy snapshot is fire-and-forget — no ACK, no retry, no resend-on-request
issues.description: The coop protocol relies on exactly-once delivery of mp:coop:enemy_state for every encounter boundary. multiplayerCoopSync.ts:516 shows the host emits this as a single transport.send() with no delivery confirmation. Contrast this with mp:lobby:start, which went through the BUG 23/H5 hardening to add a retry-every-750ms + seed-ACK handshake. The same treatment was not applied to the FIRST post-lobby message, which is arguably more critical — lobby-start retries for 3s, but if the initial enemy_state is lost, the encounter silently desyncs for the entire run with no recovery mechanism.

bestEvidence.compareTo: multiplayerLobbyService.ts:1200+ startGame() — H5 ACK handshake with 750ms retry loop and 3s timeout
bestEvidence.asymmetry: Most critical MP-state message (initial enemy snapshot) has weakest delivery guarantee in the whole stack

issues.suggestedFix: Wrap mp:coop:enemy_state in an ACK-tracked envelope. Host buffers latest snapshot keyed by encounterId; guest emits mp:coop:request_initial_state on mount if no snapshot in 2s; host replies with buffered snapshot on demand. Same pattern already used by lobby-start (H5) and lobby-settings (resend on request).

issues.title: RL reward signal missing comboCount bonus since TurnState field was removed — all trained models are undertrained on charge-correct behavior
issues.description: The `calculateCombatReward` function in gym-server.ts awards `0.1 * ts.comboCount` for each correct charge. Since `comboCount` was undefined throughout all training runs (the field was absent from TurnState), JavaScript evaluated this as `0.1 * undefined = NaN`, which JSON-serializes as `null`. The Python env received `null` reward and raised TypeError on every correct charge, causing the training process to either crash or fall back to a default. This means ALL trained models (v2_500k, v2_2M, v2_masked_2M, v3_economy_2M) were trained with corrupted reward signals for the most important positive feedback loop in the game — rewarding correct charges and chain building. The models' learned strategies are therefore unreliable with respect to charge-vs-quick decisions and chain building behavior.

bestEvidence.metric: gym-server.ts:628 ts.comboCount was undefined throughout all training; reward for correct charges was NaN (null) for all 2M-step training runs

reproductionSteps: Inspect tests/playtest/rl/analyze.py reward signal: calculateCombatReward at line 628
Confirm ts.comboCount is undefined in current TurnState (grep src/services/turnManager.ts for comboCount — returns no results)
The training logs in tests/playtest/rl/logs/ would show reward curves; if they exist, look for abnormally low reward per correct charge step
issues.resolvedBy: gym-server infra — RL reward signal is training infrastructure, not game code

issues.title: Dead guard in mp:coop:enemy_state handler — malformed payloads pass through to subscribers
issues.description: Line 209 of multiplayerCoopSync.ts reads `if (!snapshot?.currentHP === undefined || !snapshot?.maxHP === undefined) return;`. Operator precedence makes this `(!snapshot?.currentHP) === undefined`, which is always `false` (a bool compared to undefined via ===). The `||` right-hand side has the same bug. Net effect: the early-return NEVER fires. Any malformed payload — `{}`, `{currentHP: null}`, `{currentHP: 'oops'}` — passes straight through to every subscribed callback. On Steam P2P, message framing bugs or truncated payloads would be silently swallowed into subscriber state instead of being rejected at the handler boundary. Almost certainly intended as `if (snapshot?.currentHP === undefined || snapshot?.maxHP === undefined) return;`.
issues.suggestedFix: Remove the bang, and also add typeof-number check: `if (typeof snapshot?.currentHP !== 'number' || typeof snapshot?.maxHP !== 'number') return;` — matches the stricter guard pattern already used by the enemy_hp_update handler at line 219.

issues.title: SteamP2PTransport buffered-send retry budget (3 attempts at 0/200/500ms) is insufficient for slow lobby joins
issues.description: BUG 25 (commit 82bf261ef) fixed the preSendBuffer/preConnectBuffer flushing dropped-sends-with-resolved-false, routing both buffers through _sendWithRetry with 3 attempts at 0/200/500ms. However, Steam P2P session establishment time is NOT bounded at 500ms on real networks — cold-start P2P with NAT traversal and relay fallback can take 1-3 seconds on consumer home routers. After the third failed attempt, the send is silently dropped with only a diagnostic line to debug.log. The affected messages include the critical mp:lobby:start sequence and the initial mp:coop:enemy_state. There is no application-visible indication of the drop — neither player sees an error, the game silently continues with partial state.

bestEvidence.realistic: Steam P2P session establishment on NAT-challenged networks: 1-3s
bestEvidence.failureMode: Silent drop after 3rd failure; diagnostic only in ~/Library/Logs/Recall Rogue/debug.log

issues.suggestedFix: Exponential backoff to 3-5s total window (e.g., 0/200/500/1500/3000ms, 5 attempts). Surface final failures to application layer via onSendFailed callback. Show toast/overlay to players when a critical message fails to deliver so they can retry rather than suffer silent desync.

issues.title: No end-to-end test covers lobby→coop→first-encounter round-trip under SteamP2PTransport
issues.description: multiplayerLobbyService.test.ts has 43 tests — all use a MockTransport. multiplayerCoopSync.test.ts exercises barrier/delta merging in isolation. No test binds these together with a real (or realistically-mocked) SteamP2PTransport that models (a) send/receive latency asymmetry, (b) subscription-after-send races, (c) buffered-send retry budgets, (d) session priming state, (e) cross-process message framing. All of BUG 22-27 could have been caught by such a test. The user's primary bug would also have been caught. Writing it is a force-multiplier — it retroactively validates every recent fix and future-proofs the coop path.

bestEvidence.wouldCatch: BUG 22 (lobbyUpdate), 23 (onGameStart), 24 (stale lobby chat), 25 (buffered-send drop), 27 (gameScreens), and the primary user-reported issue

issues.suggestedFix: Write a Vitest integration suite that spins up two MultiplayerLobbyService instances sharing a fake SteamP2PTransport with configurable latency, send-drop rate, and session-priming delay. Assert: both clients reach combat with identical enemy snapshot, damage dealt by player A propagates to player B within 1s, turn-end barrier resolves with both deltas merged.
issues.deferredReason: Deferred to separate session — full Vitest integration harness with simulated SteamP2PTransport is multi-hundred-line effort that would dwarf the primary fix in scope. Track as its own feature.

issues.title: relicAcquisitionService.ts:140 shouldDropRandomRelic uses unseeded Math.random()

issues.title: Host never calls hostNextQuestion or hostEndGame — no question-dispatch driver exists
issues.description: initTriviaGame and initTriviaMessageHandlers are both called at game start, but no code in CardApp or any caller subsequently calls hostNextQuestion(...) or hostEndGame(). Host stalls at phase: 'waiting' indefinitely. The service exposes the full flow contract but nothing drives it forward from the caller side.
reproductionSteps: static-analysis only — grep hostNextQuestion returns zero call sites outside the test file.

issues.title: initTriviaGame called without factPool — H3/H4 dedup guard permanently disabled
issues.description: initTriviaGame(triviaPlayers, DEFAULT_ROUNDS, isLocalHost) omits the factPool argument. Per triviaNightService.ts:234 a missing factPool sets _factPool = null, disabling dedup entirely. Questions can repeat within the same game; the pool-exhausted path can never fire.
reproductionSteps: static-analysis only.

issues.title: Duel turnTimer field declared/cleared but never set — auto-resolve timeout never arms
issues.description: DuelSession.turnTimer is initialized to null and cleaned up in destroyDuel, but no assignment _duelState.turnTimer = setTimeout(...) anywhere in the file. If one player disconnects after submitting their action, the host holds localAction/opponentAction indefinitely with no fallback. Duel softlocks until both players quit.
reproductionSteps: Start a real-time duel → player A submits cards → player B drops connection → turn never resolves.

issues.title: initGameMessageHandlers not called for same_cards mode — fork-seed mp:sync handler never registers
issues.description: initGameMessageHandlers(lobby.mode) called only when lobby.mode === 'coop' || 'duel'. For same_cards it is skipped. But initGameMessageHandlers for same_cards is the only place the mp:sync fork-seed handler is registered (multiplayerGameService.ts:1000). Guest never receives host fork seeds → all RNG forks (card pools, enemy intents, shop rolls) diverge from turn 1.
reproductionSteps: Create a same_cards lobby → host broadcasts fork seeds → guest has no mp:sync handler → players see different card rewards/enemies.

issues.title: same_cards winner determination — true ties always award win to local player
issues.description: enemy_defeated branch sets winnerId = p1Total >= p2Total ? localPlayerId : opponentId — when totals equal, local player always wins. No multi-axis tie-breaker (floor/accuracy/duration) like determineRaceWinner. Both clients independently evaluate this and each considers themselves winner in true tie.
reproductionSteps: static-analysis only — requires equal total damage at enemy defeat.

issues.title: Race progress broadcast closure always sends host's player ID — guest progress mis-attributed
issues.description: In startRaceProgressBroadcast's getProgress closure, localId is derived as lobby?.players.find(p => p.isHost)?.id. On the guest client, p.isHost is false, so find returns the host's ID instead of guest's. Every progress/finish packet the guest sends carries the host's playerId — host appears with two entries, guest with none.
reproductionSteps: Join a race as guest; opponent HUD shows two identical player IDs on results screen.

issues.title: Opponent disconnect mid-race causes permanent softlock — results never emit
issues.description: _tryEmitRaceResults requires both _localProgress.isFinished AND _opponentProgress.isFinished. If opponent disconnects before finishing, mp:race:finish never sent, _opponentProgress stays isFinished:false, _onRaceComplete never fires. Local player who finished is trapped on final encounter screen with no exit.
reproductionSteps: Start a race, finish all floors, opponent closes the game — local player has no way to exit.

issues.title: Map node consensus blocks race progress — race incorrectly waits for both players to pick same node
issues.description: initMapNodeSync called unconditionally for all MP modes at line 940; handleMapNodeSelect routes through pickMapNode for any isMultiplayerRun. Race is parallel-run mode where each player navigates independently, but consensus callback at 983-988 blocks room transition until opponent picks same node. A racer who clicks node A is blocked until opponent also clicks node A.
reproductionSteps: In a race, click a map node — room transition hangs waiting for opponent consensus that won't come.

issues.title: Late-joining guest sees empty _picks map — single inbound message instantly triggers consensus
issues.description: initMapNodeSync seeds _picks from getCurrentLobby() at init time. If a guest joins after host already called initMapNodeSync (after run starts), guest's _picks only contains their own entry. When guest receives mp:map:node_pick from host, a new key is added dynamically (line 61), then consensus check at 157-165 only compares the two entries rather than against expected lobby roster. One inbound message instantly triggers consensus even if a third player hasn't picked.
reproductionSteps: static-analysis — 3-player lobby where host picks before guest 2 joins.

issues.title: Rest site overlay reads playerHp/playerMaxHp from one prop — doesn't label which player's HP

issues.title: Coop mystery event 'choice' branch — each player resolves own sub-effect with no consensus

issues.title: Coop room exits (shop/rest/mystery) go directly to dungeonMap with no coop barrier

issues.title: Coop falls through to live FSRS writes — both peers write to own DB for SAME shared encounter

issues.title: Attempt A (2-peer Docker lockstep) not feasible — BroadcastChannel transport cannot cross container boundaries
issues.description: The multiplayer transport implementation (multiplayerTransport.ts) uses BroadcastChannel for peer-to-peer messaging. BroadcastChannel is a browser same-origin API — messages only reach tabs within the same browser context. Two separate Docker containers each have isolated Chromium instances. No cross-container BroadcastChannel is possible without a shared WebSocket server or similar bridge. Therefore, a true 2-peer headless lockstep test cannot be conducted with the current infrastructure. The test was downgraded to Attempt B (single-peer seed determinism via Vitest).

bestEvidence.mechanism: BroadcastChannel API — tab-scoped, not cross-process or cross-container

reproductionSteps: Spin up two Docker containers
Attempt to have both join the same BroadcastChannel
Messages will not be received across containers
issues.resolvedBy: BroadcastChannel transport limitation documented, not a game bug

issues.title: gameScreens allowlist in CardApp.svelte is hand-enumerated — silent leaveLobby() risk on future screen additions
issues.description: BUG 27 (commit 61a60edd9) grew the gameScreens Set from 7 entries to ~20 after discovering that navigating to any in-run screen not in the allowlist would silently call leaveLobby() and tear down multiplayer state mid-run. The fix typed the Set as `Set<Screen>` so stale references fail compilation, but the set is still hand-enumerated. Adding a new in-run screen (e.g., future boss-gauntlet, new reward type) requires the author to remember to update gameScreens — if they forget, the same mid-run teardown regression ships silently.

bestEvidence.residualRisk: Next Screen added to the Screen union without adding to gameScreens will trigger mid-run leaveLobby()

issues.suggestedFix: Invert the logic: maintain `mpScreens` (lobby-ish) and `hubScreens` (outside-run) Sets, and treat everything else as implicitly in-run. Or derive gameScreens from a Screen-union exclusion (a `Exclude<Screen, HubScreen | LobbyScreen>` computed at compile time so adding a new Screen can't silently opt out of the game-screen allowlist). Add a lint (scripts/lint/check-gamescreens-allowlist.mjs) that diffs the Screen union against the Set literal and fails if any Screen is missing.
issues.deferredReason: BUG 27 fix (commit 61a60edd9) already mitigates runtime risk by typing the Set as Set<Screen>. Compile-derived Exclude<> type + lint script is hardening nice-to-have, not a bug.

issues.title: No player-visible diagnostic when coop sync silently fails mid-run
issues.description: The MpDebugOverlay UI was removed 2026-04-22 (commit 82bf261ef). All diagnostics now route to ~/Library/Logs/Recall Rogue/debug.log on macOS (%LocalAppData%/Recall Rogue/debug.log on Windows) or require opening devtools and calling window.__rrMpDebug(). A Steam player experiencing the primary bug has no in-game indication that sync failed — they see drift but the game pretends everything is fine. This is exactly how the user discovered the bug: visual divergence, no error state. Recommend a subtle HUD indicator (color-dot on the multiplayer HUD) that turns yellow when no partner heartbeat for >15s and red when critical messages have failed delivery.

Click to open debug overlay with full transport state. Reuse the multiplayerCoopSync partner-heartbeat tracker that already exists (lines 55-130).

issues.title: Production CSP hardcoded to *.recallrogue.com — blocks any future WebSocket/HTTP fallback on Steam build
issues.description: vite.config.ts:31 sets production CSP to `connect-src 'self' https://*.recallrogue.com https://localhost:*`. Steam P2P goes through Tauri IPC (not connect-src), so current Steam builds are unaffected. However, this creates a trap: if future work tries to fall back from Steam P2P to a WebSocket relay (for NAT-unfriendly networks, debug mode, LAN play without manual config), the CSP will block the connection and the fallback will fail silently with a console error invisible to the player. Also blocks any future in-game telemetry, error reporting, or live-ops messaging that points anywhere but recallrogue.com.

bestEvidence.deadCode: src/csp.ts exports getCSPMeta() — never imported, actual CSP is the vite plugin

issues.suggestedFix: Either (a) make the production CSP Tauri-aware: detect __TAURI_INTERNALS__ at HTML-injection time and use a permissive desktop CSP that allows tauri://, steam://, and relay/LAN endpoints; or (b) document that any non-Steam-P2P networking requires a CSP exception and add a lint that catches new connect-src-consuming code without a matching CSP update. Delete the dead csp.ts module regardless (lint flagged it already in MP-20260413-003941-MP-012).
issues.deferredReason: CSP rework is risky surface area — wants user sign-off on which endpoints (wss://mp.recallrogue.com? steam://? relay fallback?) to allow before editing the prod CSP.
issues.note: [2026-04-23] vite.config.ts CSP hardcode unchanged; docs-only update adds trap section + action checklist

issues.title: pickBackend() re-runs platform detection on every lookup — not cached, not single-source-of-truth
issues.description: multiplayerTransport.ts:1850 and multiplayerLobbyService.ts:1850 both re-evaluate `window.__TAURI_INTERNALS__ || window.__TAURI__` on every call. If these globals are ever inconsistently present (injection timing on slow cold starts, page navigations that unload/reload the bridge, Tauri v2 quirks), different callers within the same session could receive different transport/backend pairings — the lobby service thinking it's on Steam while the transport factory returns WebSocket, or vice versa. This is unlikely under current Tauri v2 behavior but represents an invariant that is not enforced by the code.

bestEvidence.pattern: duplicate isTauriRuntime() / tauriPresent checks in two modules

issues.suggestedFix: Unify on src/services/platformService.ts — it already captures `const isDesktop = platform === 'desktop'` at module-load time. Have both pickBackend implementations import and use that single constant instead of re-sniffing globals. Alternatively memoize the result of the first check and expose as a module-level const.

issues.title: DEBUG_COOP=false hardcoded — most coop-sync tracing is silent, requires rebuild to enable
issues.description: multiplayerCoopSync.ts:39 sets `const DEBUG_COOP = false;` as a module-level constant. The coopLog helper (line 41) no-ops when false. Only 3 of ~20 trace points were promoted to rrLog (which does ship to debug.log on Steam) as part of BUG 27. Everything else — turn-end barrier state, delta collection, partner-state subscription firing, enemy-hp-update receipt, solo-survivor handling — is invisible unless the developer rebuilds with DEBUG_COOP=true. On a Steam player's machine, that's never an option. Deep-debugging a desync in the wild is impractical.

bestEvidence.coopLogSites: ~20 call sites, 3 promoted to rrLog by BUG 27

issues.suggestedFix: Replace DEBUG_COOP with a runtime-toggleable flag. Either (a) localStorage-based: `window.__rrSetMpDebug(true)` writes a flag that coopLog reads on each call; (b) always-on rrLog with a per-category filter applied by the log reader; (c) tie to URL param `?debug=mp` or keyboard combo. Log-on-demand beats log-at-compile-time for shipped code.

issues.title: _session_request_callback and _lobby_enter_callback in SteamState stored as Option<()> — callback objects may be GC'd after SteamState::new() returns
issues.description: src-tauri/src/steam.rs SteamState struct stores callback handles as Option<()> (unit type). Depending on steamworks-rs 0.12 API, register_callback returns a drop-guard handle that must be retained for the lifetime of the callback. Storing unit instead of the actual handle type means Drop fires immediately after SteamState::new() returns, potentially unregistering the callbacks.
issues.suggestedFix: Store the actual returned handle type (likely CallbackHandle or similar) from steamworks-rs register_callback to ensure Drop fires on app exit, not after new().
issues.note: [2026-04-23] Documented as upstream steamworks-rs 0.12.2 bug; Option<()> unchanged — behavior still broken

issues.title: LobbyChatUpdate.Entered arm in steam.rs does not filter by active_lobby_id — stale Entered events from prior lobby may trigger auto-accept sends
issues.description: src-tauri/src/steam.rs:245-263: BUG 24 added active_lobby_id filtering to the Left/Disconnected/Kicked/Banned arms but not to the Entered arm. A stale Entered event from a lobby the player already left would trigger an auto-accept zero-byte send to a peer who is no longer in the same lobby. Probably harmless but adds log noise and potentially reactivates a stale P2P session.

bestEvidence.bug24: filtered Left/Disconnected/Kicked/Banned but not Entered arm

issues.suggestedFix: Add `if lobby_id != active_lobby_id { return; }` guard to the Entered arm, matching the pattern applied to other arms in BUG 24.

issues.title: notifyLobbyUpdate early-returns when no subscribers — unnecessarily lossy, next update re-emits but creates a window of stale state
issues.description: src/services/multiplayerLobbyService.ts:1345: if the subscriber set is empty, notifyLobbyUpdate returns early instead of being a no-op iteration. While functionally equivalent today (no subscriber, no effect), it discards state changes that might be needed if a subscriber registers between the state change and the next notification.
issues.suggestedFix: Drop the early-return guard. Let Set iteration over zero subscribers be a natural no-op.

issues.title: notifyLobbyUpdate snapshot shares players array reference across subscribers in same fire — future mutation in one subscriber corrupts others
issues.description: src/services/multiplayerLobbyService.ts:1357: the snapshot object passed to each subscriber shares the same players array reference. CardApp re-spreads defensively today, but a future subscriber that mutates in place would corrupt others' view of the same fire.

bestEvidence.risk: shared players array reference across subscriber calls

issues.suggestedFix: Deep-clone the players array per subscriber call, or Object.freeze the snapshot to make in-place mutation a hard error.

issues.title: peerPresenceMonitor initialization failures not surfaced — misconfigured monitor silently does nothing
issues.description: src/services/multiplayerTransport.ts initPeerPresenceMonitor: if the monitor fails to initialize, the error is not emitted to onLobbyError or logged visibly. Peer presence detection silently fails, leaving the session with no disconnect detection until the 45s barrier timeout.
issues.suggestedFix: Emit console.warn on init failure and fire onLobbyError so the application layer knows presence detection is unavailable.

issues.title: setActiveLobby on SteamP2PTransport declared but never called — disconnect() never fires via setActiveLobby path

issues.suggestedFix: Either wire setActiveLobby from the lobby service on lobby join, or delete the dead code path.

issues.title: MultiplayerHUD and PlayerRosterPanel may unmount/remount mid-run if currentLobby transiently nulls (BUG 27 family)

If currentLobby ever transiently nulls while in a gameScreen (e.g., during a lobby service state reset), both components unmount and remount, losing any local display state and potentially causing visible flicker.

issues.title: _preConnectBuffer cap of 64 with FIFO eviction may drop first mp:lobby:settings under high UI interaction before guest arrives
issues.description: SteamP2PTransport's _preConnectBuffer has a 64-entry cap with FIFO eviction. The first settings broadcast (the most critical for lobby state bootstrap) can be evicted if the host interacts with the UI enough before the guest arrives, generating more than 64 subsequent settings updates. The guest then receives only partial settings history.
issues.suggestedFix: Prioritize protocol-critical messages (start, settings, join) over chatty ones (ping) in the eviction strategy. Never evict the first settings broadcast. Or raise cap to 256.
issues.note: [2026-04-23] protected preSendBuffer eviction; cap 64→256

issues.title: VITE_MP_WS_URL defaults to ws://localhost:3000/mp/ws with no Steam-build compile-time override
issues.description: multiplayerTransport.ts:56 defines `const DEFAULT_MP_WS_URL = 'ws://localhost:3000/mp/ws';`. In a Steam production build, import.meta.env.VITE_MP_WS_URL is whatever was set at `npm run build` time inside `scripts/steam-*.sh`. If that env var is missing (it's not in vite.config.ts, not in a committed .env, not in tauri.conf.json), every Steam player's WebSocket fallback points at ws://localhost:3000 on their OWN machine — which has no Fastify server. Steam currently avoids this path entirely (SteamP2PTransport wins on Tauri), but any future LAN-toggle or WebSocket-fallback code path falls into this trap.

bestEvidence.envFiles: .env / .env.local (dev only, not bundled into Steam binary)

issues.suggestedFix: Either (a) fail-fast: if production build detects Tauri runtime AND WebSocketTransport is selected AND VITE_MP_WS_URL is unset, log an error and disable the fallback rather than silently pointing at localhost; or (b) set VITE_MP_WS_URL=wss://mp.recallrogue.com (or whatever the prod relay is) in scripts/steam-build.sh. Document the intended production URL in docs/architecture/multiplayer.md.
issues.deferredReason: Decision depends on whether MP ever needs a WebSocket fallback on Steam builds (currently pure P2P). Ties into broader MP relay infrastructure call.
issues.note: [2026-04-23] DEFAULT_MP_WS_URL localhost unchanged; steam-build.sh no VITE_MP_WS_URL override; docs-only

issues.title: No unit test guarantees mp:lobby:start always carries contentSelection payload

The CardApp guard `if (!lobby.contentSelection) { transitionScreen('multiplayerLobby'); return; }` is defensive but fires AFTER the user clicks start — by then the damage is done (the player is bounced back to lobby). A regression in startGame() that forgets contentSelection would ship without test failure unless the exact shape is asserted.

bestEvidence.missingCase: parameterized sweep of all 5 modes × {contentSelection present / missing / partial} with startGame() shape assertions

issues.suggestedFix: Add a parameterized test that calls startGame() under every MultiplayerMode with and without contentSelection and asserts the exact transport.send payload shape. Also add a type-level test (or a .d.ts assertion) that LobbyStartPayload.contentSelection is required, not optional.
issues.deferredReason: Narrow regression test — will add alongside the broader integration harness in MP-006.

issues.title: H18 solo-survivor wiring TODO at encounterBridge never resolved
issues.description: multiplayerCoopSync.ts lines 23-28 carry an explicit `TODO(H18-wiring): call handleCoopPlayerDeath from encounterBridge.ts applyDamageToLocalPlayer (or equivalent death-check site) when run.multiplayerMode === 'coop' && newHp <= 0`. The handleCoopPlayerDeath function exists (line ~629) but its caller never got wired. Design intent: when one coop player's HP reaches 0, encounter ends immediately for ALL coop players and both receive a loss. Current behavior: dead player sits on game-over screen while living partner continues solo until their own HP drops or they win. Not a ship-blocker, but a stated design invariant that's unenforced.

bestEvidence.callSiteMissing: src/services/encounterBridge.ts applyDamageToLocalPlayer does not invoke it

issues.suggestedFix: In encounterBridge.ts wherever player HP commits after enemy phase (grep for `player.hp = ` or `applyDamageToLocalPlayer`), add `if (run.multiplayerMode === 'coop' && newHp <= 0) handleCoopPlayerDeath(localPlayerId);`. Add a test that two-player coop with one player at 1HP ends for both when the enemy lands a hit.

issues.title: TriviaRoundScreen 'waiting' phase has no UI branch — blank screen with no escape
issues.description: The {#if ... question} / {:else if ... revealing} / {:else if ... finished} chain falls through to empty render with no back/return button. Combined with TN-002 (no question dispatch driver), the host is stuck in 'waiting' permanently and the screen is blank.
reproductionSteps: Observe screen after initTriviaGame is called before any hostNextQuestion fires.
issues.note: [2026-04-23] [ui-cleanup-wave-2 fix] Added {:else} branch to TriviaRoundScreen phase chain. Shows "Waiting for the next round." microcopy (phase-specific ternary), three-dot pulse animation, and Return to Hub escape button with data-testid="trivia-waiting-escape". Satisfies .claude/rules/ui-layout.md softlock-prevention rule.

issues.title: resetMapNodePicks not called on lobby leave — defensive cleanup gap
issues.description: destroyMapNodeSync clears _picks and nulls _cleanupListener; resetMapNodePicks is never called during lobby teardown. Currently safe because initMapNodeSync calls destroyMapNodeSync first, but any path that calls initMapNodeSync without first destroying would carry stale picks into the new session.
reproductionSteps: static-analysis only.

issues.title: No analytics events for race completion, ELO update, or season score submission
issues.description: analyticsEvents.ts defines CoopEvent and partial SocialEvent but contains no race_finished, elo_updated, or season_score_submitted events. ELO changes at race/duel end produce no analytics signal — rating volatility and matchmaking fairness can't be tracked.
reproductionSteps: static-analysis only.

issues.title: No game-version check when listing/joining lobbies — cross-version joins silently proceed
issues.description: LobbyBrowserEntry has no gameVersion field. joinLobbyById never compares client version to lobby's server version. LAN discovery returns version field from /mp/discover but lobby browser never checks. Player on v1.2 can join v1.3 host with no warning, causing desync.
reproductionSteps: static-analysis only.

issues.title: LAN discovery uses HTTP subnet scan (no mDNS) — silently fails on isolation networks
issues.description: Comment explicitly acknowledges mDNS not implemented (TODO: Consider Tauri mDNS/Bonjour). Current HTTP-sweep polls compact IP set (.1–.32, .100–.110, .200–.210, .254) and misses hosts outside those ranges. Access-point isolation (common on managed/public Wi-Fi) blocks all cross-client unicast probes — LAN discovery silently returns zero results with no user-facing explanation.
reproductionSteps: static-analysis only.

issues.title: CSP blocks Docker containers from connecting to Fastify server
issues.description: The Vite plugin in vite.config.ts hardcodes the dev CSP connect-src without http://host.docker.internal:*. Docker containers running the game cannot reach the Fastify multiplayer server at host.docker.internal:3000. CSP errors block all REST and WebSocket connections.
issues.resolvedBy: Added http://host.docker.internal:* to dev CSP connect-src in vite.config.ts line 29

issues.title: CORS rejects Docker origin for Fastify multiplayer API
issues.description: Fastify server defaults CORS_ORIGIN to http://localhost:5173, but Docker containers connect from http://host.docker.internal:5173. CORS preflight fails with 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin header has a value http://localhost:5173 that is not equal to the supplied origin'.
issues.resolvedBy: Must start Fastify with CORS_ORIGIN='http://localhost:5173,http://host.docker.internal:5173'. Skill spec should document this.

issues.title: Lobby state persists across navigation - auto-reconnects on multiplayerMenu return
issues.description: Once a player connects to a lobby, navigating away (hub) and back to multiplayerMenu auto-reconnects to the old lobby. The player sees lobby elements (lobby-code, player-slots, btn-ready) instead of the mode selection menu (tab-create, tab-join, mode cards). navigate('hub') does not call leaveLobby() on the lobby service. This makes it impossible to join a different lobby without restarting the container.
issues.note: [2026-04-23] [2026-04-23 verify] CardApp.svelte:820 polarity-inverted lobbyTerminatingScreens clears currentLobby on multiplayerMenu return

issues.description: Guest's Ready button click does not toggle ready state. Button stays showing 'Ready' (not toggled) even after multiple clicks via Playwright click and eval-dispatched pointer events. Host's Ready button toggles correctly to 'Not Ready'. Start gate remains disabled because guest is never ready. This completely blocks two-player race starts.

issues.title: Player slot count inconsistency between Fastify and host UI
issues.description: Fastify REST API reports currentPlayers: 2 but host UI only renders 1 player slot (Player 1 only, no Player 2). The guest appears in the server state but not in the host's DOM. Possibly a WebSocket state sync issue where the guest's join message doesn't update the host's reactive player list.

issues.title: Leave lobby button click stuck on loading screen
issues.description: Clicking btn-leave-lobby triggers a page transition but gets stuck on a loading screen (three dots animation on dark background). The DOM still reports lobby elements (lobby-code, player-slots) but the visual state is a blank loading screen. eval-clicking the button has the same result.

issues.title: Race enterRoom() from dungeon map leads to black screen
issues.description: After starting a race (lobby -> onboarding -> dungeonMap), calling enterRoom() or selectMapNode() transitions to a black screen with screen: 'unknown' and inCombat: false. The Phaser scene transition fails silently. No console errors. The game is completely stuck.

issues.title: Race start shows onboarding instead of directly entering dungeon
issues.description: Starting a race from a lobby (with bot + deck selected + ready) shows the onboarding 'RECALL ROGUE - Enter the depths' splash screen before the dungeon map. This adds a mandatory click-through step. In multiplayer, both players should enter the dungeon simultaneously without an intro screen.

issues.title: Deck picker 'Nothing selected' after clicking deck row
issues.description: In the LobbyDeckPicker, clicking a deck row (e.g., 'Mammals of the World') does not visually select it. The picker footer still shows 'Nothing selected'. The correct interaction is to click the ldp-deck-check-btn checkbox button next to the deck, not the row itself.

issues.title: Error details button visible in lobby UI
issues.description: The lobby screen contains a 'Restart Game' button and an 'Error details' collapsible at the bottom of the DOM. These appear to be dev/error-state elements that are rendered even when no error has occurred. They may not be visible to the player (could be hidden via CSS) but are present in the DOM.

issues.title: Start button shows 'Select Content' before deck is chosen
issues.description: The btn-start-game button text shows 'Select Content' instead of 'Start Game' when no deck has been selected. After deck selection it changes to 'Start Game'. Then after guest joins but guest not ready, it shows 'Start Game' but disabled. These state transitions work but the 'Select Content' label is confusing as a start button label.
